PROFESSOR VIEW 01 / 28
BUSINESS & LEGAL ENGLISH — 40 Aulas

Rafael Gasparello Lima

De "enferrujado e me virando" a executivo que conduz reuniões, viagens e missões em inglês

São Paulo, SP A2 Advogado / Diretor 60 min Online
PROGRESS0%
ALUNO
Rafael Gasparello Lima
IDADE
47 anos
CIDADE
São Paulo, SP
PROFISSÃO
Advogado, Diretor e Conselheiro — Grupo Picunha
NÍVEL
A2 (Pré-Intermediário)
FOCO
Business English + Legal English
FREQUÊNCIA
2x/semana (seg e qua, 20h) — 60 min
TOTAL
40 aulas — Online

Jornada de Transformação

Um advogado e diretor de 47 anos com inglês "enferrujado", que se vira em viagens mas não consegue conduzir reuniões, negociações ou missões profissionais em inglês. Confunde tempos verbais básicos e evita situações onde precisa falar.
↓
Um executivo confiante que conduz reuniões com parceiros internacionais, apresenta sua empresa em board calls, viajá com a família para Disney em fevereiro de 2027 falando inglês com fluência, e cumpre a missão que seu chefe lhe deu.

"Em 40 aulas, Rafael vai sair do 'enferrujado e me virando' para executivo que conduz reuniões, viagens e missões em inglês com autonomia e confiança."

Forças

  • Motivação clara — missão do chefe + viagem Disney com filho
  • Experiência prévia com inglês — não parte do zero absoluto
  • Perfil analítico de advogado — entende lógica gramátical
  • Disciplina — jiu-jítsu 2x/semana mostra comprometimento
  • Contexto rico — ferro, agro, conselho, jurídico = vocabulário diverso

Fraquezas

  • Confunde Present Simple com Past Simple (erro fossilizado)
  • Nível A2 — vocabulário limitado para contexto executivo
  • Inglês "enferrujado" — gap entre o que sabe e o que produz
  • Pouca prática oral — evita situações em inglês
  • Rotina intensa — precisa de aulas objetivas e eficientes

Currículo Completo — 40 Aulas

#TemaFoco Linguístico
01The Comeback — Who Is Rafael in English?Present Simple reactivation; business vocab
02My Company in Detail — Describing OperationsPresent Simple routines; industry vocabulary
03Daily Routine — A Day in Rafael's LifePresent Simple + frequency adverbs; daily activities
04First Contact — Meeting Colleagues and ClientsCan/Could for requests; introductions
05The Office — Workplace and TeamThere is/are; prepositions; office vocabulary
06Review & Consolidation — Lessons 1-5All structures reviewed; fluency check
07Business Emails — Writing Clear MessagesEmail phrases; imperatives; formal register
08On the Phone — Calls and Video ConferencesTelephone/video call language; Can/Could
09Numbers and Data — Reports and FiguresNumbers; comparatives; financial basics
10At the Airport — Travel EssentialsModal verbs; travel vocabulary
11Hotel and Restaurant — Travel CommunicationOrdering; polite requests; directions
12Review & Consolidation — Lessons 7-11All structures; mid-program assessment
13What Happened? — Describing Past EventsPast Simple regular verbs
14Company History — Milestones and ChangesPast Simple irregular verbs
15Experience — What Have You Done?Present Perfect introduction
16Board Meetings — Opinions and DecisionsOpinion phrases; agree/disagree
17Legal Basics — Contracts and TermsLegal vocabulary; must/have to
18Review & Consolidation — Lessons 13-17All structures reviewed
19Negotiations I — Making ProposalsWould/Could for proposals; conditionals (1st)
20Negotiations II — Reaching AgreementsAgreeing/disagreeing; compromise language
21Reports — Presenting InformationPassive voice basic; data vocabulary
22Problems — When Things Go WrongShould/shouldn't; problem-solving language
23Culture — Brazilian vs American BusinessComparatives/superlatives; cultural vocabulary
24Review & Consolidation — Lessons 19-23All structures reviewed
25Future Plans — Strategy and GoalsGoing to/will; planning vocabulary
26Projects — Deadlines and DeliverablesBy/until/before/after; project vocabulary
27Presentations I — Opening and StructureDiscourse markers; presentation phrases
28Presentations II — Data and ClosingLinking words; visual data language
29Client Relationships — Building TrustPresent Perfect + for/since; relationship vocab
30Review & Consolidation — Lessons 25-29All structures; advanced assessment
31Legal Meetings — Due DiligenceSpecialized legal terms; formal language
32Closing Deals — Final NegotiationsConditional 2nd; deal-closing language
33International Travel — Advanced SituationsReported speech basic; travel problem-solving
34Crisis Communication — Managing IssuesAdvanced modals; crisis vocabulary
35Leadership — Inspiring and DelegatingPersuasion vocabulary; leadership language
36Review & Consolidation — Lessons 31-35All structures reviewed
37Disney Prep — Travel with FamilyTravel + family vocabulary; practical phrases
38Conferences — Networking and PresentingComplex introductions; networking language
39Final Assessment — Show What You KnowComprehensive role-play; all structures
40Celebration — From Rusty to ReadyGraduation; reflection; next steps

Metodologia

Task-Based Learning

Cada aula gira em torno de uma tarefa real do universo de Rafael: apresentar-se em board call, descrever sua empresa, conduzir reunião. A língua é ferramenta, não fim.

Grammar Discovery

Rafael descobre regras a partir de exemplos contextualizados. Nunca regra antes do uso. Indutivo sempre.

Spaced Repetition

Vocabulário retorna em warm-ups, diálogos e exercícios de aulas posteriores. Revisão orgânica e cumulativa.

Scaffolded Production

De guided a semi-free a free. Rafael sempre tem apoio antes de voar sozinho.

Delayed Error Correction

Erros são anotados durante production e corrigidos depois, sem interromper a fluência.

Mapa de Personalidade

RITMO
Direto e objetivo. Executivo ocupado com rotina intensa. Aulas precisam ser eficientes e práticas.
MOTIVAÇÃO
Dupla: missão profissional do chefe + viagem Disney com filho de 6 anos. Motivação intrínseca e extrínseca.
ESTILO
Analítico (advogado). Gosta de entender a lógica por trás da regra. Gramática explícita funciona bem após discovery.
TOLERÂNCIA AO ERRO
Moderada. Sabe que está "enferrujado" e aceita o processo, mas pode frustrar com erros recorrentes.
CONTEXTO IDEAL
Board calls, reuniões jurídicas, viagens internacionais, apresentações corporativas.
TOM DO PROFESSOR
Respeitoso e profissional. Rafael é executivo sênior de 47 anos, ex-aluno Alumni. Tom de parceria, nunca condescendente.
ALERTAS
Erro fossilizado: usa Past Simple para rotinas ("When I woke up..." em vez de "When I wake up..."). Corrigir com paciência e repetição constante.

Bem-vindo, Rafael!

Este é o seu material de Pre-class. Complete os exercícios antes de cada aula para aproveitar ao máximo o tempo com seu professor.

"Meu chefe me deu uma missão nova, que é precisar do idioma."

Frases de Emergência

Could you repeat that, please?
Poderia repetir, por favor?
I am sorry, I did not understand.
Desculpe, não entendi.
Could you speak more slowly, please?
Poderia falar mais devagar, por favor?
What does that mean?
O que isso significa?
Aula 01 — Pre-class

The Comeback

Who Is Rafael in English? — Present Simple reactivation
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra, ouça a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

Board of Directors•conselho de administração
A group of people who control a company
"I am a member of the board of directors."
Headquarters•sede/matriz
The main office of a company
"Our headquarters is in São Paulo."
To manage•gerenciar
To be in charge of something
"I manage the legal department of the group."
Industry•setor/indústria
A type of business activity
"The iron industry is our main business."
Meeting•reunião
A time when people come together to talk
"I have a meeting with my boss every Monday."
Role•cargo/função
A person's job or position in a company
"My role includes legal and corporate duties."
To report to•reportar-se a
To have someone as your boss
"I report to the CEO of the group."
Subsidiary•subsidiária/filial
A company controlled by a bigger company
"We have subsidiaries in agriculture and iron."

Vocabulary Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra em inglês com a tradução correta em português.

Board of Directors
Headquarters
To manage
Industry
Meeting
Role
To report to
Subsidiary

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o texto abaixo. As estruturas gramáticais estão em negrito. Depois, responda as perguntas.

Rafael Gasparello Lima is a lawyer, director, and board member at Grupo Picunha in São Paulo. He works in a large economic group that operates in two main industries: iron manufacturing and agriculture. Rafael manages the legal department and also serves on the board of directors. He reports to the CEO.

Every day, Rafael wakes up early, takes his son to school, and trains jiu-jitsu before work. He goes to the office and attends meetings throughout the day. Twice a week, he trains jiu-jitsu again in the evening.

Rafael is studying English again because his boss needs him to use the language at work. He travels to the United States once a year, and he wants to communicate better in professional situations.
1. What does Rafael do at Grupo Picunha?
A He is an accountant
B He manages the legal department and serves on the board of directors
C He is the CEO of the company
2. Why is Rafael studying English?
A He wants to move to the United States
B His boss needs him to use English at work
C He wants to become an English teacher
3. Which verb tense is used for Rafael's routines in the text?
A Present Simple (works, manages, wakes, trains)
B Past Simple (worked, managed, woke, trained)
C Future (will work, will manage)

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Revise o Present Simple — como falar de rotinas e fatos.

Present Simple — Rotinas e Fatos
SUBJECT AFFIRMATIVE NEGATIVE QUESTION
I/You/We/TheyI work every day.
Eu trabalho todo dia.		I don't work on Sundays.
Eu não trabalho aos domingos.		Do you work here?
Você trabalha aqui?
He/She/ItHe works every day.
Ele trabalha todo dia.		He doesn't work on Sundays.
Ele não trabalha aos domingos.		Does he work here?
Ele trabalha aqui?

Signal words: every day, always, usually, twice a week, once a year, on Mondays

✗ "When I woke up, I take my son to school." (ERRADO — passado para rotina)

✓ "When I wake up, I take my son to school." (CORRETO — presente para rotina)

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases com a palavra ou forma verbal correta.

"I in a large economic group called Picunha."
"Rafael the legal department."
"Our company in two industries: iron and agriculture."
"I to the CEO of the group."
"He English very often at work."
" Rafael travel to the United States every year?"
"Our is in São Paulo."
"I am a member of the ."

Order Stage 2: Self-Introduction Order

Organize as frases na ordem lógica para uma apresentação pessoal profissional.

?"Our company operates in the iron industry and agriculture."
?"It is a pleasure to meet you."
?"Good morning. My name is Rafael Gasparello Lima."
?"I manage the legal department and serve on the board of directors."
?"I am a lawyer and director at Grupo Picunha in São Paulo."

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouça cada frase, grave sua voz e compare a pronúncia.

My name is Rafael Gasparello Lima. I am a lawyer and director.
I work in a large economic group called Picunha.
Our headquarters is in São Paulo, but we operate in two industries.
I report to the CEO and serve on the board of directors.
Could you repeat that, please?

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação profissional.

You meet a new international colleague at a conference. How do you introduce yourself?
A "Hey, I do law stuff in Brazil."
B "Good morning. My name is Rafael Gasparello Lima. I am a lawyer and director at Grupo Picunha in São Paulo."
C "I am Rafael. I have a company."
Your boss asks you in English: "What does your company do?" What do you say?
A "We operated in iron and agriculture." (past tense)
B "Our company operates in two main industries: iron manufacturing and agriculture."
C "We are do many things."
Someone speaks too fast in a meeting. What do you say?
A "I don't understand your English."
B Nothing — just nod.
C "Could you repeat that, please? I want to make sure I understand correctly."
Which sentence correctly describes your daily routine?
A "When I woke up, I take my son to school." (past for routine)
B "When I wake up, I take my son to school." (present simple)
C "When I am waking up, I am taking my son." (continuous for routine)

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente. Não há certo ou errado — o objetivo é práticar.

Imagine you are in a video call with an international partner. They ask: "Tell me about yourself and your company." Record your answer in English. Try to include: your name, your role, your company, what it does, and where it is located.

Survival Card — Lesson 1

1My name is Rafael Gasparello Lima. I am a lawyer and director.Meu nome é Rafael Gasparello Lima. Eu sou advogado e diretor.
2I work at Grupo Picunha. We operate in iron and agriculture.Eu trabalho no Grupo Picunha. Operamos em ferro e agricultura.
3Our headquarters is in São Paulo.Nossa sede é em São Paulo.
4I report to the CEO and serve on the board of directors.Eu me reporto ao CEO e faço parte do conselho.
5Could you repeat that, please?Poderia repetir, por favor?

Checklist — Aula 1

  • Estudei as 8 palavras de vocabulário
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o texto de Grammar in Context
  • Revisei o Grammar Tip (Present Simple)
  • Completei os fill-in-the-blanks
  • Organizei a self-introduction
  • Pratiquei a pronúncia (5 frases)
  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 02 — Pre-class

My Company in Detail

Describing Operations — Present Simple 3rd Person
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra, ouça a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

Department•departamento
A part of a company that does a specific job
"Rafael works in the legal department."
To supply•fornecer
To give something that is needed
"Our company supplies iron to other businesses."
Manufacturing•fabricação/manufatura
The process of making products in a factory
"The iron manufacturing division is the biggest."
Revenue•receita/faturamento
The money a company earns from business
"Our revenue comes from two main industries."
Employee•funcionário
A person who works for a company
"The group has over five hundred employees."
Client•cliente
A person or company that buys your services
"We have clients in Brazil and overseas."
Partnership•parceria
A business relationship between companies
"We have partnerships with international firms."
To expand•expandir
To grow bigger or increase in size
"The company wants to expand operations overseas."

Vocabulary Expressions

Expressões úteis para descrever sua empresa.

Be based in•ser sediado em
"Our company is based in São Paulo."
Focus on•focar em
"The legal department focuses on contracts."
Deal with•lidar com
"I deal with corporate governance issues."

Vocabulary Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra em inglês com a tradução correta em português.

Department
To supply
Manufacturing
Revenue
Employee
Client
Partnership
To expand

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o texto abaixo. As estruturas gramáticais estão em negrito. Depois, responda as perguntas.

Grupo Picunha is a large economic group based in São Paulo. The company operates in two main industries. The first division manufactures and supplies iron to construction companies across Brazil. The second division focuses on agriculture — it produces fruits and meat for domestic and international markets.

The group has over five hundred employees and generates significant revenue from both divisions. Rafael works in the legal department, but he also deals with corporate governance. The company doesn't export directly to the United States yet, but it has partnerships with international firms. The CEO wants to expand operations overseas, and that is why Rafael needs to improve his English.
1. What does the first division do?
A It produces fruits and meat
B It manufactures and supplies iron to construction companies
C It exports products to the United States
2. Does the company export directly to the United States?
A Yes, it exports every month
B No, but it has partnerships with international firms
C Yes, it exports iron to New York
3. What happens to verbs when the subject is he/she/it/the company?
A The verb gets -s/-es/-ies (operates, manufactures, supplies, has)
B The verb stays the same (operate, manufacture, supply)
C The verb uses -ing (operating, manufacturing)

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Present Simple — 3rd Person Rules (he/she/it/the company)

Present Simple — 3rd Person Rules
RULE EXAMPLE
Most verbs: add -swork → works, operate → operates
Verbs ending in -ch, -sh, -s, -x, -o: add -esgo → goes, do → does
Consonant + y: change y to -iessupply → supplies, study → studies
Irregular: have → hasThe company has 500 employees.
Negative: doesn't + base formHe doesn't export directly.
Question: Does + subject + base form?Does the company have offices overseas?

✗ "The company supply iron to clients." (ERRADO — falta -ies)

✓ "The company supplies iron to clients." (CORRETO)

✗ "The group have many employees." (ERRADO — have para 3rd person)

✓ "The group has many employees." (CORRETO)

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases com a palavra ou forma verbal correta.

"The company iron products."
"Our agricultural division fruits and meat."
"The group over five hundred employees."
"Rafael with corporate governance issues."
"The company directly to the United States."
" the legal department focus on contracts?"
"Our comes from two main industries."
"We have with international companies."

Order Stage 2: Describing Your Company

Organize as frases na ordem lógica para descrever sua empresa.

?"We have over five hundred employees."
?"We are based in São Paulo, Brazil."
?"We are looking to expand our operations overseas."
?"Our company is called Grupo Picunha."
?"The group operates in two industries: iron and agriculture."

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouça cada frase, grave sua voz e compare a pronúncia.

Our company manufactures iron and supplies it to construction businesses.
The group has over five hundred employees across Brazil.
We have partnerships with international firms.
The company doesn't export directly, but we want to expand.
Our revenue comes from iron manufacturing and agriculture.

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação profissional.

An international partner asks: "What does your company do?" What is the best answer?
A "We did many things in Brazil." (past tense)
B "Our company operates in two industries: iron manufacturing and agriculture. We supply iron and produce fruits and meat."
C "We are a company." (too vague)
Your CEO asks you to describe the company structure in English. Which sentence is correct?
A "The group have five hundred employees." (have instead of has)
B "The group has over five hundred employees across two divisions."
C "The group having many peoples." (grammar error)
Someone asks: "Does your company export?" What do you say?
A "No, the company doesn't export directly, but we have partnerships with international firms."
B "No, we don't exports." (double marking)
C "We are not export." (wrong structure)
Which sentence correctly uses 3rd person Present Simple?
A "The company supply iron to clients." (no -ies)
B "The company supplies iron to clients across Brazil."
C "The company is supply iron." (wrong structure)

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente. Não há certo ou errado — o objetivo é práticar.

Imagine you are preparing a short presentation about Grupo Picunha for an international investor meeting. Describe your company: what it does, how many employees it has, what industries it operates in, and its plans for the future. Record your answer in English.

Survival Card — Lesson 2

1Our company operates in two industries: iron and agriculture.Nossa empresa atua em dois setores: ferro e agricultura.
2The group has over five hundred employees.O grupo tem mais de quinhentos funcionários.
3We supply iron to construction companies across Brazil.Fornecemos ferro para empresas de construção em todo o Brasil.
4We have partnerships with international firms.Temos parcerias com empresas internacionais.
5The company wants to expand operations overseas.A empresa quer expandir operações no exterior.

Aula 03 — Pre-class

A Day in Rafael's Life

Daily Routine — Present Simple + Frequency Adverbs
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra, ouça a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

Commute•deslocamento/trajeto
The trip from home to work
"Rafael's commute to the office takes about thirty minutes."
Schedule•agenda/cronograma
A plan for when things will happen
"My schedule is very busy during the week."
Deadline•prazo
The last day to finish something
"I have an important deadline on Friday."
Lunch break•intervalo de almoço
The time during work to eat lunch
"I usually have lunch during my lunch break at 12."
To attend•participar/comparecer
To go to a meeting or event
"I attend meetings every morning."
Routine•rotina
The things you do every day in the same order
"My routine starts at 6 a.m."
Appointment•compromisso/consulta
A planned meeting at a specific time
"I have an appointment with a client at 3 p.m."
Overtime•hora extra
Extra time worked after normal hours
"I rarely work overtime, but this week is different."

Vocabulary Expressions

Expressões úteis para descrever sua rotina.

Wake up early•acordar cedo
"I always wake up early to take my son to school."
Work out•treinar/malhar
"Rafael works out twice a week. He trains jiu-jitsu."
Pick up (someone)•buscar (alguém)
"I pick up my son from school at 5 p.m."

Vocabulary Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra em inglês com a tradução correta em português.

Commute
Schedule
Deadline
Lunch break
To attend
Routine
Appointment
Overtime

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o texto abaixo. Os advérbios de frequência estão em negrito. Depois, responda as perguntas.

Rafael always wakes up early. He usually gets up at 6 a.m. and takes his son to school before work. After that, he often trains jiu-jitsu in the morning — he goes to training twice a week. Then he has lunch and commutes to the office.

At work, Rafael attends meetings throughout the day. He usually has a busy schedule with many deadlines. He sometimes works late when there is an important case, but he rarely works overtime. He never works on weekends because he values family time.

In the evening, Rafael sometimes trains jiu-jitsu again. On other nights, he picks up his son, has dinner with his family, and relaxes at home. His routine is structured and predictable — he likes it that way.
1. How often does Rafael wake up early?
A He always wakes up early
B He sometimes wakes up early
C He never wakes up early
2. Does Rafael work on weekends?
A Yes, he usually works on Saturdays
B No, he never works on weekends
C Yes, he always works on Sundays
3. Where do the frequency adverbs (always, usually, never) appear in the sentence?
A Before the main verb (e.g., He always wakes up)
B After the main verb (e.g., He wakes always up)
C At the beginning of the sentence (e.g., Always he wakes up)

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Frequency Adverbs — Position in the Sentence (Advérbios de Frequência — Posição na Frase)

Frequency Adverbs — Scale & Position
ADVERB FREQUENCY EXAMPLE
always100%I always wake up early.
usually80%I usually have lunch at 12.
often60%He often attends meetings.
sometimes40%I sometimes work late.
rarely10%She rarely works overtime.
never0%I never work on weekends.

Position Rule (Regra de posição):

✓ Before the main verb: I always wake up early.

✓ After TO BE: He is always on time.

✗ NUNCA no início (diferente do português): Always I wake up early. → I always wake up early.

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases com a palavra ou advérbio correto.

"I wake up at 6 a.m."
"Rafael takes his son to school before work."
"He trains jiu-jitsu in the morning."
"I work late when there is an important case."
"She works ."
"I work on weekends."
"My to the office takes about thirty minutes."
"I have a busy with many ."

Order Stage 2: Rafael's Morning Routine

Organize as frases na ordem correta da rotina matinal do Rafael.

?"I train jiu-jitsu at the gym."
?"I attend meetings and work until the evening."
?"I wake up at 6 a.m. and get ready."
?"I have lunch before going to the office."
?"I take my son to school."

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouça cada frase, grave sua voz e compare a pronúncia.

I always wake up early and take my son to school.
My commute to the office takes about thirty minutes.
I usually attend meetings in the morning.
I rarely work overtime, but I sometimes work late.
I never work on weekends because I value family time.

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

A colleague asks: "What time do you usually get to the office?" What is the best answer?
A "I get usually to the office at 9." (wrong adverb position)
B "I usually get to the office around 9 a.m."
C "Usually I am getting to the office." (wrong structure)
Your boss asks: "Do you work on weekends?" What do you say?
A "No, I never work on weekends."
B "No, never I work on weekends." (wrong adverb position)
C "No, I am never working on weekends." (wrong — continuous for routine)
Someone asks: "How often do you exercise?" Best answer:
A "I work out twice a week. I train jiu-jitsu on Mondays and Wednesdays."
B "I am working out two times." (wrong tense)
C "I work out all days." (wrong — every day, not all days)
Which sentence has the correct adverb position?
A "I attend always meetings in the morning." (wrong position)
B "Always I attend meetings in the morning." (wrong position)
C "I always attend meetings in the morning."

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente. Não há certo ou errado — o objetivo é práticar.

Describe your daily routine in English. Start from when you wake up and end with your evening. Try to use at least 4 frequency adverbs (always, usually, sometimes, never). Record your answer.

Survival Card — Lesson 3

1I always wake up early and take my son to school.Eu sempre acordo cedo e levo meu filho à escola.
2My schedule is very busy during the week.Minha agenda é muito cheia durante a semana.
3I usually attend meetings in the morning.Eu geralmente participo de reuniões pela manhã.
4I rarely work overtime.Raramente faço hora extra.
5I never work on weekends.Eu nunca trabalho nos finais de semana.

Checklist — Aula 3

  • Estudei as 8 palavras de vocabulário
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o texto de Grammar in Context

  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 04 — Pre-class

First Contact — Meeting Colleagues and Clients

Social/Business Vocabulary — Can/Could for Requests, Offers, and Ability
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra, ouca a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

Colleague•colega de trabalho
A person you work with
"I have a meeting with my colleague from the legal team."
To introduce•apresentar
To tell someone another person's name
"Let me introduce you to our CEO."
Pleased to meet you•prazer em conhecê-lo
A polite way to greet someone new
"Pleased to meet you. I am Rafael."
Business card•cartão de visita
A small card with your contact information
"Here is my business card."
To shake hands•apertar as mãos
To hold and move someone's hand when you meet
"In business, we always shake hands."
Available•disponível
Free to do something or meet someone
"Are you available for a meeting tomorrow?"
To arrange•agendar/organizar
To plan or organize something
"Can we arrange a meeting for next week?"
Contact information•informações de contato
Phone number, email, and address
"Could you send me your contact information?"

Vocabulary Expressions

Expressões essenciais para networking profissional.

Nice to meet you•prazer em conhecê-lo
"Nice to meet you. I have heard great things about your work."
Looking forward to•ansioso por / na expectativa de
"I am looking forward to working with you."
Get in touch•entrar em contato
"Let us get in touch next week to discuss the project."

Practice Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra a sua tradução correta.

To arrange
Contact information
Colleague
Available
Pleased to meet you
To shake hands
To introduce
Business card

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas de compreensão.

Rafael is at a business conference in São Paulo. He can see many professionals from different industries. He can speak some English, but he wants to improve. A woman approaches him.

"Good morning. My name is Karen Williams. I am from New York. Can I ask what company you are with?"
"Of course. I am Rafael Gasparello Lima. I work at Grupo Picunha. Could I give you my business card?"
"Thank you. Could you tell me more about your company?"
"Sure. We operate in iron manufacturing and agriculture. Can we arrange a meeting to discuss a possible partnership?"
"Could you send me an email with your contact information?"
"Of course. I can send it today. I am looking forward to working with you."
1. What does Rafael offer Karen?
A His phone number
B His business card
C His email address
2. What does Karen ask Rafael to send?
A A contract
B A report about his company
C An email with his contact information
3. Which word is more polite: CAN or COULD?
A Can (is more polite)
B Could (is more polite)
C They are the same

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip — Can / Could

Entenda quando usar CAN e COULD em pedidos, ofertas e habilidades.

Use Can (informal) Could (more polite)
Request (pedido)Can you repeat that?Could you repeat that, please?
Offer (oferta)Can I help you?Could I give you my card?
Ability (habilidade)I can speak English.I could understand him yesterday.
Permission (permissao)Can I sit here?Could I ask you a question?

Diferenca principal:

  • Can = informal, cotidiano — Pode/Posso
  • Could = mais educado, profissional — Poderia
  • Em contextos de negocios, prefira Could para pedidos
  • Estrutura: Can/Could + sujeito + verbo base (SEM "to")
✗ "Could you to send me an email?"
✓ "Could you send me an email?"

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases com a palavra correta.

" I ask what company you are with?"
" you tell me more about your company?"
"I speak English, but I want to improve."
" I give you my business ?"
" we arrange a for next week?"
" you send me your ?"
"Let me you to our CEO."
"Are you for a meeting tomorrow?"

Order Stage 2: Put in Order

Organize as frases na ordem correta de uma apresentação profissional.

?"Can we arrange a meeting to discuss a partnership?"
?"Pleased to meet you. I work at Grupo Picunha."
?"I am looking forward to working with you."
?"Good morning. My name is Rafael Gasparello Lima."
?"Could I give you my business card?"

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouca, grave e compare sua pronúncia.

Pleased to meet you. I am Rafael Gasparello Lima.
Could you tell me more about your company?
Can we arrange a meeting for next week?
I am looking forward to working with you.
Could you send me your contact information?

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

You meet a new international partner at a conference. What do you say?
A "Hey, what's up?" (too informal for business)
B "Good morning. Pleased to meet you. I am Rafael from Grupo Picunha."
C "I am Rafael. Here is my card." (too abrupt)
You want to politely ask someone to repeat what they said. Best option:
A "What did you say?" (too direct)
B "Could you repeat that, please?"
C "Can you to say that again?" (wrong: "to" after can)
You want to offer your business card. Most polite option:
A "Take my card." (too direct)
B "Could I give you my business card?"
C "Can I to give you my card?" (wrong: "to" after can)
Which sentence is grammatically correct?
A "Could you to send me an email?" (wrong: "to" after could)
B "Could you send me an email with your contact information?"
C "Could you sending me an email?" (wrong: -ing after could)

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente.

Imagine you are at a business conference in Miami. You meet an American professional. Introduce yourself, ask about their company, offer your business card, and arrange a future meeting. Use Can and Could. Record your answer.

Survival Card — Lesson 4

1Pleased to meet you. I am Rafael Gasparello Lima.Prazer em conhecê-lo. Eu sou Rafael Gasparello Lima.
2Could I give you my business card?Posso lhe dar meu cartão de visita?
3Could you tell me more about your company?Poderia me contar mais sobre sua empresa?
4Can we arrange a meeting for next week?Podemos agendar uma reunião para a próxima semana?
5I am looking forward to working with you.Estou ansioso para trabalhar com você.

Checklist — Aula 4

  • Estudei o vocabulário
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o texto e respondi o quiz
  • Entendi a gramática (Can/Could)
  • Completei os fill-in-the-blank
  • Pratiquei a pronúncia
  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 05 — Pre-class

The Office — Workplace and Team

Office/Workplace Vocabulary — There is / There are + Prepositions of Place
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra, ouca a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

Office•escritório
The place where you work
"My office is on the third floor."
Conference room•sala de reunião
A large room for meetings
"There is a conference room on every floor."
Floor•andar
A level of a building
"The legal department is on the fifth floor."
Printer•impressora
A machine that prints documents
"There is a printer next to my desk."
Reception•recepção
The area where visitors arrive
"The reception is on the ground floor."
Parking lot•estacionamento
A place to park your car
"There is a parking lot behind the building."
Elevator•elevador
A machine that moves people between floors
"The elevator is next to the stairs."
Cafeteria•refeitório/cantina
A place in a building where you can eat
"There is a cafeteria on the first floor."

Vocabulary Expressions

Expressões essenciais para descrever locais no escritório.

On the (first/second) floor•no (primeiro/segundo) andar
"The legal department is on the fifth floor."
Next to•ao lado de
"My office is next to the conference room."
In front of•em frente a
"The reception desk is in front of the elevator."

Practice Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra a sua tradução correta.

Conference room
Elevator
Parking lot
Office
Printer
Floor
Reception
Cafeteria

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o texto e responda as perguntas de compreensão.

Grupo Picunha's headquarters is in São Paulo. There is a modern building with ten floors. There are over five hundred employees working there every day.

On the ground floor, there is a reception area and a large parking lot behind the building. There is an elevator next to the stairs. The cafeteria is on the first floor — there are many tables and a coffee machine.

Rafael's office is on the fifth floor. There is a conference room next to his office, and there is a printer in front of the elevator. There are three meeting rooms on his floor. The CEO's office is on the tenth floor, above all the departments.

There aren't any international offices yet, but there is a plan to open one in the United States.

1. How many floors does the building have?
A Five floors.
B Ten floors.
C Three floors.
2. Where is the cafeteria?
A On the ground floor.
B On the first floor.
C On the fifth floor.
3. Where is the parking lot?
A Behind the building.
B In front of the building.
C Next to the elevator.
4. Does the company have international offices?
A Yes, there are many.
B No, but there is a plan to open one.
C Yes, in the United States.

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Estude a estrutura gramátical da aula.

There is / There are

Usamos there is para coisas no singular e there are para coisas no plural.

SingularPlural
AffirmativeThere is a conference room.There are three meeting rooms.
NegativeThere isn't an office overseas.There aren't any problems.
QuestionIs there a cafeteria? Yes, there is.Are there any meeting rooms? Yes, there are.
Prepositions of Place (Preposições de lugar)
  • in — dentro de: The printer is in the office.
  • on — sobre/em: My office is on the fifth floor.
  • at — em (local): I am at the reception.
  • next to — ao lado de: The elevator is next to the stairs.
  • between — entre: The cafeteria is between the lobby and the parking lot.
  • in front of — em frente a: The desk is in front of the window.
  • behind — atras de: The parking lot is behind the building.

Atenção: Em português dizemos "Tem uma sala de reunião" usando o verbo TER. Em inglês, NUNCA use "It has" para descrever existência. Use sempre There is / There are.

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Complete as frases com a palavra correta.

"There a conference room on every floor."
"There three meeting rooms on my floor."
" there a cafeteria in the building?"
"There any international offices yet."
"My office is the fifth floor."
"The printer is my desk."
"The parking lot is the building."
"The reception is the elevator."

Order Stage 2: Ordering

Coloque as frases na ordem correta para dar direções no prédio.

?"My office is on the fifth floor."
?"The cafeteria is on the first floor if you need coffee."
?"Welcome to Grupo Picunha. This is the reception."
?"There is a conference room next to my office."
?"The elevator is next to the stairs, on your right."

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouca a frase, grave sua voz e compare.

There is a conference room on every floor.
My office is on the fifth floor, next to the elevator.
There are over five hundred employees in this building.
The parking lot is behind the building.
Is there a cafeteria on this floor?

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

1. A visitor asks: "Where is the conference room?" Best answer:
A "There is on the third floor."
B "The conference room is on the third floor, next to the elevator."
C "Conference room is third floor."
2. Someone asks: "Is there a parking lot?" What do you say?
A "Yes, there is. It is behind the building."
B "Yes, it has."
C "Yes, there are a parking lot."
3. Your colleague asks: "How many meeting rooms are there on your floor?" Best answer:
A "There are three meeting rooms on my floor."
B "There is three meeting rooms."
C "Have three meeting rooms."
4. Which sentence is correct?
A "There is many employees in the building."
B "There are many employees in the building."
C "It has many employees in the building."

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Descreva seu prédio para um colega novo. Inclua: quantos andares há, onde fica seu escritório, onde fica a sala de reunião, onde fica o refeitório e onde fica o estacionamento. Use There is/There are e preposições. Grave sua resposta.

Describe your office building to a new colleague. Include: how many floors there are, where your office is, where the conference room is, where the cafeteria is, and where the parking lot is. Use There is/There are and prepositions. Record your answer.

Survival Card — Lesson 5

1My office is on the fifth floor.Meu escritório é no quinto andar.
2There is a conference room next to my office.Há uma sala de reunião ao lado do meu escritório.
3There are three meeting rooms on this floor.Há três salas de reunião neste andar.
4The cafeteria is on the first floor.O refeitório fica no primeiro andar.
5Is there a parking lot? Yes, it is behind the building.Tem estacionamento? Sim, fica atrás do prédio.

Checklist — Aula 5

  • Estudei o vocabulário
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o texto e respondi o quiz
  • Entendi a gramática (There is/There are)
  • Completei os fill-in-the-blank
  • Pratiquei a pronúncia
  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 06 — Pre-class

Putting It All Together

Review & Consolidation — Lessons 1-5
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Review

Revisão das palavras-chave das aulas 1-5. Ouça a pronúncia e releia o exemplo.

Headquarters•sede/matriz
The main office of a company
"Our headquarters is in São Paulo."
Subsidiary•subsidiária/filial
A company controlled by a bigger company
"We have subsidiaries in agriculture."
Revenue•receita/faturamento
The money a company earns
"Our revenue comes from two divisions."
Partnership•parceria
A business relationship between companies
"We have partnerships with international firms."
Schedule•agenda/cronograma
Your plan of activities and times
"My schedule is very busy during the week."
Commute•trajeto/deslocamento
Your trip from home to work
"My commute takes about thirty minutes."
Conference room•sala de conferência
A room where people have meetings
"There is a conference room next to my office."
Parking lot•estacionamento
An area where people park their cars
"There is a large parking lot behind the building."

Vocabulary Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra em inglês com a tradução correta em português.

Headquarters
Subsidiary
Revenue
Partnership
Schedule
Commute
Conference room
Parking lot

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o texto. As quatro estruturas das aulas 1-5 estão em negrito. Depois, responda as perguntas.

Today an international partner, Sarah, visits Grupo Picunha. Rafael is a director and he manages the legal department. The company operates in two industries, and its revenue comes from both divisions. Rafael usually attends meetings in the morning, and he rarely works overtime.

When Sarah arrives, Rafael says: "Could you tell me the topics for the meeting?" and "Can we arrange it for the afternoon?" Then he gives a tour. "There is a conference room next to my office," he says, "and there are three meeting rooms on this floor. There is a large parking lot behind the building."
1. How often does Rafael work overtime?
A Always
B Rarely
C Every weekend
2. Which sentence is a polite request?
A "The company operates in two industries."
B "Could you tell me the topics for the meeting?"
C "There are three meeting rooms."
3. Why does Rafael say "There are three meeting rooms" (not "There is")?
A Because "meeting rooms" is plural
B Because it is a question
C Because it is in the past

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Revise as quatro ferramentas gramaticais das aulas 1-5 em uma só tabela.

As Quatro Ferramentas (Lessons 1-5)
TOOL USO EXEMPLO
Present SimpleRotinas e fatos
(he/she/it + verbo + s)		He works. / She manages.
Frequency adverbsCom que frequência
(antes do verbo)		I usually work. / He rarely travels.
Can / CouldPedidos
(Could = mais formal)		Could you help me? / Can we meet?
There is / There areDescrever lugares
(is = singular, are = plural)		There is a room. / There are rooms.

✗ "When I woke up, I take my son to school." (ERRADO — passado para rotina)

✓ "When I wake up, I take my son to school." (CORRETO — presente para rotina)

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases misturando as quatro estruturas das aulas 1-5.

"Our company in two industries."
"I attend meetings in the morning."
" you send me the topics by email?"
"There a conference room next to my office."
"There three meeting rooms on this floor."
"He on weekends."
"Our comes from two divisions."

Order Stage 2: Hosting a Partner

Organize as frases na ordem lógica para receber uma parceira internacional. Ouça primeiro.

?"Our company operates in iron and agriculture."
?"There is a conference room next to my office."
?"Good morning, Sarah. Welcome to Grupo Picunha."
?"Could we arrange a meeting this afternoon?"
?"I am a lawyer and director here."

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouça cada frase, grave sua voz e compare a pronúncia.

I am a lawyer and director at Grupo Picunha. We operate in iron and agriculture.
Our headquarters is in São Paulo, and our revenue comes from two divisions.
I usually wake up early and attend meetings all morning.
Could you send me the topics by email?
There is a conference room next to my office on the fifth floor.

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação profissional.

A partner asks what your company does. What do you say?
A "We operated in iron and agriculture." (past)
B "We operate in two industries: iron manufacturing and agriculture."
C "We are operate in iron."
You want to politely ask a partner to send a document. What do you say?
A "Could you send me the document, please?"
B "You send me the document now."
C "Send document you can?"
A visitor asks about meeting rooms on your floor. What do you say?
A "There is three meeting rooms."
B "There are three meeting rooms on this floor."
C "It has three meeting rooms."
How do you correctly describe your morning routine?
A "When I woke up, I take my son to school." (past for routine)
B "I usually wake up early and take my son to school."
C "I am wake up early every day."

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente. Não há certo ou errado — o objetivo é praticar.

An international partner is visiting your office for the first time. Record a short audio in English: welcome her, introduce yourself and your company, describe your morning routine, make a polite request (arrange a meeting), and describe your office using "There is / There are."

Survival Card — Lesson 6

1I am a lawyer and director at Grupo Picunha. We operate in iron and agriculture.Sou advogado e diretor no Grupo Picunha. Operamos em ferro e agricultura.
2Our headquarters is in São Paulo, and our revenue comes from two divisions.Nossa sede é em São Paulo, e nossa receita vem de duas divisões.
3I usually wake up early and attend meetings all morning.Eu geralmente acordo cedo e participo de reuniões a manhã toda.
4Could you send me the topics by email?Você poderia me enviar os tópicos por e-mail?
5There is a conference room next to my office on the fifth floor.Há uma sala de conferência ao lado do meu escritório no quinto andar.

Checklist — Aula 6

  • Revisei as 8 palavras de vocabulário
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o texto de Grammar in Context
  • Revisei o Grammar Tip (as 4 ferramentas)
  • Completei os fill-in-the-blanks
  • Organizei a sequência de hosting
  • Pratiquei a pronúncia (5 frases)
  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 07 — Pre-class

Business Emails

Writing Clear Messages — email phrases, imperatives, formal register
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra de e-mail, ouça a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

Attachment•anexo
A file you send with an email
"Please find the report in the attachment."
Recipient•destinatário
The person who receives the email
"Add the recipient before you send it."
Subject line•linha de assunto
The title that says what the email is about
"Write 'Contract Review' in the subject line."
To reply•responder
To answer an email
"I will reply to your message today."
To forward•encaminhar
To send a received email to someone else
"I will forward it to my legal team."
Regards•atenciosamente
A polite word to close an email
"Best regards, Rafael."
Draft•rascunho
A version of an email before you send it
"I will write a draft and check it first."
To follow up•dar seguimento
To send another message to check progress
"I will follow up next week."

Vocabulary Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra em inglês com a tradução correta em português.

Attachment
Recipient
Subject line
To reply
To forward
Regards
Draft
To follow up

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o e-mail. As frases de e-mail estão em negrito. Depois, responda as perguntas.

Dear Ms. Collins,

I am writing to share my comments on the contract. Please find my notes attached. Could you forward this email to your legal team? Please let me know if you have any questions.

I look forward to your reply.

Best regards,
Rafael Gasparello Lima
1. Why is Rafael writing this email?
A To cancel a meeting
B To share his comments on the contract
C To ask for a job
2. Which phrase sends a file?
A "Please find my notes attached."
B "I look forward to your reply."
C "Best regards."
3. How does Rafael close the email?
A "Bye!"
B "Best regards, Rafael Gasparello Lima"
C "See you"

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

As partes de um e-mail profissional e o registro formal.

Email Phrases & Register
PARTE FRASE (FORMAL)
Greeting
(saudação)		Dear Mr. / Ms. + sobrenome,
Reason
(motivo)		I am writing to + verbo...
Request
(pedido)		Please find... / Could you...?
Close
(encerramento)		I look forward to your reply.
Best regards, + nome

✗ "I wait your reply." (tradução literal)

✓ "I look forward to your reply." (frase fixa correta)

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases de e-mail.

" Ms. Collins,"
"I am to share my comments."
"Please find the report ."
"Could you this to your team?"
"Please if you have any questions."
"I look to your reply."
"Best , Rafael."

Order Stage 2: Order the Email

Organize as partes na ordem correta de um e-mail profissional. Ouça primeiro.

?"Please find my notes attached."
?"Best regards, Rafael Gasparello Lima."
?"Dear Ms. Collins,"
?"Could you reply by Friday?"
?"I am writing to share my comments on the contract."

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouça cada frase, grave sua voz e compare a pronúncia.

Dear Ms. Collins, I am writing to share my comments.
Please find the report attached.
Could you reply by Friday, please?
Please let me know if you have any questions.
I look forward to your reply. Best regards, Rafael.

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação de e-mail.

You want to send a contract with your email. What do you write?
A "I send you the contract in attachment."
B "Please find the contract attached."
C "Contract is here."
How do you politely ask a partner to answer by Friday?
A "Could you reply by Friday, please?"
B "Reply Friday."
C "You must answer Friday."
Which is the best formal closing?
A "Bye, Rafael"
B "Best regards, Rafael Gasparello Lima"
C "Thx"
You have not received a reply. What do you write?
A "Why you not answer me?"
B "I am writing to follow up on my last email."
C "Answer please now."

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente. Não há certo ou errado — o objetivo é praticar.

Record a short email out loud in English to an international partner. Include: a greeting (Dear...), your reason for writing (I am writing to...), one request (Could you... / Please find... attached), and a professional close (I look forward to your reply. Best regards...).

Survival Card — Lesson 7

1Dear Ms. Collins, I am writing to share my comments.Prezada Sra. Collins, escrevo para compartilhar meus comentários.
2Please find the report attached.Segue o relatório em anexo.
3Could you reply by Friday, please?Você poderia responder até sexta, por favor?
4Please let me know if you have any questions.Por favor, me avise se tiver dúvidas.
5I look forward to your reply. Best regards, Rafael.Aguardo seu retorno. Atenciosamente, Rafael.

Checklist — Aula 7

  • Estudei as 8 palavras de e-mail
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o e-mail de Grammar in Context
  • Revisei o Grammar Tip (partes do e-mail)
  • Completei os fill-in-the-blanks
  • Organizei as partes do e-mail
  • Pratiquei a pronúncia (5 frases)
  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 08 — Pre-class

On the Phone

Calls & Video Conferences — phone language + Can/Could
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra de telefone/vídeo, ouça a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

To call back•retornar a ligação
To return a phone call later
"I will call you back this afternoon."
To hold on•aguardar na linha
To wait on the line for a moment
"Could you hold on a moment, please?"
Voicemail•caixa postal de voz
A system to leave a recorded message
"I left a message on her voicemail."
To mute•silenciar o microfone
To turn your microphone off
"Please mute your microphone when you are not speaking."
To unmute•reativar o som
To turn your microphone back on
"Let me unmute. Can you hear me now?"
Connection•conexão
How well your phone or internet works
"Sorry, the connection is bad."
To hang up•desligar
To end a phone call
"Let us not hang up until we finish."
To dial•discar
To press the numbers to make a call
"I dial the number to call the office."

Vocabulary Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra em inglês com a tradução correta em português.

To call back
To hold on
Voicemail
To mute
To unmute
Connection
To hang up
To dial

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o texto. As frases de telefone estão em negrito. Depois, responda as perguntas.

Rafael's phone rings at 9 a.m. He answers: "Hello, this is Rafael Gasparello Lima speaking." The caller asks, "Could I speak to the legal team?" Rafael says, "Could you hold on a moment, please?" and dials the extension.

Later, he joins a video call with Sarah. The connection is bad, so he says, "Sorry, can you hear me? Let me unmute." At the end, they do not hang up until everything is clear. If the call drops, Rafael will call Sarah back right away.
1. How does Rafael answer the phone?
A "Who are you?"
B "Hello, this is Rafael Gasparello Lima speaking."
C "Yes, hello?"
2. Why does Rafael say "Let me unmute"?
A Sarah cannot hear him on the video call
B He wants to end the call
C He is leaving a voicemail
3. What will Rafael do if the call drops?
A Send an email only
B Call Sarah back right away
C Wait until tomorrow

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

As frases de cada etapa de uma ligação + Can/Could.

Phone Phrases & Can/Could
ETAPA FRASE
Answer
(atender)		Hello, this is + nome + speaking.
Ask for someone
(pedir alguém)		Could I speak to + nome?
Wait
(aguardar)		Could you hold on a moment?
Audio check
(checar som)		Can you hear me? / Let me unmute.

✗ "Wait!" / "Who are you?" (direto demais)

✓ "Could you hold on, please?" / "Who is calling, please?"

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases de telefone.

"Hello, this is Rafael Gasparello Lima ."
" I speak to Ms. Collins, please?"
"Could you a moment, please?"
"I am on mute. Let me ."
"Sorry, the is bad."
"I will you back this afternoon."
" you hear me now?"

Order Stage 2: Answer the Call

Organize as frases na ordem correta para atender uma ligação. Ouça primeiro.

?"Could you hold on a moment, please?"
?"Thank you for calling. Goodbye."
?"Hello, this is Rafael speaking."
?"I will connect you to the right person."
?"How can I help you?"

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouça cada frase, grave sua voz e compare a pronúncia.

Hello, this is Rafael Gasparello Lima speaking.
Could I speak to Ms. Collins, please?
Could you hold on a moment, please?
Sorry, the connection is bad. Could you call back?
Can you hear me now? I will unmute.

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação de chamada.

You answer the office phone. What do you say?
A "Yes? Who?"
B "Hello, this is Rafael Gasparello Lima speaking."
C "Talk."
You need a moment to find a document. What do you say?
A "Could you hold on a moment, please?"
B "Wait there."
C "Stop talking."
On a video call, the other person cannot hear you. What is the likely problem?
A "I hang up."
B "I am on mute — let me unmute."
C "I dial the number."
The connection is bad. What do you say?
A "Your phone is broken."
B "Sorry, the connection is bad. Could you call back?"
C "I cannot."

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente. Não há certo ou errado — o objetivo é praticar.

Record a short phone call out loud in English. Answer professionally (Hello, this is...), ask to speak to someone or take a message, make one polite request (Could you...), and end the call politely (Thank you for calling. Goodbye).

Survival Card — Lesson 8

1Hello, this is Rafael Gasparello Lima speaking.Alô, aqui é o Rafael Gasparello Lima.
2Could I speak to Ms. Collins, please?Eu poderia falar com a Sra. Collins, por favor?
3Could you hold on a moment, please?Você poderia aguardar um momento, por favor?
4Sorry, the connection is bad. Could you call back?Desculpe, a conexão está ruim. Você poderia ligar de volta?
5Can you hear me now? I will unmute.Consegue me ouvir agora? Vou tirar do mudo.

Checklist — Aula 8

  • Estudei as 8 palavras de telefone/vídeo
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o texto de Grammar in Context
  • Revisei o Grammar Tip (frases de telefone)
  • Completei os fill-in-the-blanks
  • Organizei como atender uma ligação
  • Pratiquei a pronúncia (5 frases)
  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 09 — Pre-class

Numbers and Data

Reports & Figures — numbers, comparatives, financial basics
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra de números/dados, ouça a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

Increase•aumento
When a number goes up
"Revenue showed a strong increase."
Decrease•queda / redução
When a number goes down
"Agriculture had a small decrease."
Profit•lucro
Money the company keeps after costs
"Profit is higher than last year."
Growth•crescimento
When something gets bigger over time
"The iron division had the highest growth."
Quarter•trimestre
A period of three months
"Revenue grew this quarter."
Forecast•previsão
A prediction about future numbers
"We forecast steady growth."
Figure•número / cifra
A number, especially in a report
"All the figures are in the report."
Percentage•porcentagem
A part of one hundred (%)
"Revenue grew by twelve percent."

Vocabulary Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra em inglês com a tradução correta em português.

Increase
Decrease
Profit
Growth
Quarter
Forecast
Figure
Percentage

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o relatório. Os números e comparativos estão em negrito. Depois, responda as perguntas.

In the first quarter, Grupo Picunha had good results. Total revenue increased by twelve percent. The iron division had the highest growth, and it grew faster than agriculture. The agriculture division had a small decrease of three percent.

Profit is higher than last year. The figures are clear: this quarter is better than the last one. Rafael's forecast for the next quarter is steady growth.
1. By what percentage did revenue increase?
A Three percent
B Twelve percent
C Twenty percent
2. Which division grew more?
A The iron division (highest growth)
B The agriculture division
C Both the same
3. How does profit compare to last year?
A Lower than last year
B Higher than last year
C The same as last year

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Comparativos/superlativos e como ler números.

Comparatives, Superlatives & Numbers
USO FORMA / EXEMPLO
Comparar 2
(adjetivo curto)		adjetivo + -er than: higher than
Adjetivo longomore + adjetivo + than
O melhor/maiorthe + adjetivo + -est: the highest
Números7.5% = seven point five percent; 1,500,000 = one million five hundred thousand

✗ "more high" / "increased of 12%" (errado)

✓ "higher" / "increased by 12%" (correto)

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases com a forma correta (números e comparativos).

"Revenue by twelve percent this quarter."
"Profit is than last year."
"The iron division had the growth."
"Agriculture by three percent."
"We steady growth for the next quarter."
"This quarter is than the last one."
"Revenue grew by twelve ."

Order Stage 2: Present the Figures

Organize as frases na ordem lógica de uma apresentação de resultados. Ouça primeiro.

?"The iron division had the highest growth."
?"We forecast steady growth for the next quarter."
?"Good morning. Let me present the first quarter figures."
?"Profit is higher than last year."
?"Total revenue increased by twelve percent."

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouça cada frase, grave sua voz e compare a pronúncia.

Revenue increased by twelve percent this quarter.
The iron division had the highest growth.
Profit is higher than last year.
Agriculture decreased by three percent.
We forecast steady growth for the next quarter.

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação com números.

Revenue went up 12%. How do you report it?
A "Revenue increased of twelve percent."
B "Revenue increased by twelve percent."
C "Revenue is more big twelve percent."
Iron grew more than agriculture. How do you compare them?
A "Iron grew faster than agriculture."
B "Iron grew more fast than agriculture."
C "Iron is the more fast."
Iron is the top division. How do you say it?
A "Iron had the most high growth."
B "Iron had the highest growth."
C "Iron had the more growth."
How do you say 7.5%?
A "Seven comma five percent."
B "Seven point five percent."
C "Seven and half percents."

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente. Não há certo ou errado — o objetivo é praticar.

Record a short report out loud in English about your company's results. Give one figure (an increase or decrease with a percentage), compare two parts of the business (higher than / the highest), mention profit, and give a forecast for the next quarter.

Survival Card — Lesson 9

1Revenue increased by twelve percent this quarter.A receita aumentou doze por cento neste trimestre.
2The iron division had the highest growth.A divisão de ferro teve o maior crescimento.
3Profit is higher than last year.O lucro está mais alto do que no ano passado.
4Agriculture decreased by three percent.A agricultura caiu três por cento.
5We forecast steady growth for the next quarter.Prevemos crescimento estável para o próximo trimestre.

Checklist — Aula 9

  • Estudei as 8 palavras de números/dados
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o relatório de Grammar in Context
  • Revisei o Grammar Tip (comparativos e números)
  • Completei os fill-in-the-blanks
  • Organizei a apresentação de resultados
  • Pratiquei a pronúncia (5 frases)
  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 10 — Pre-class

At the Airport

Travel Essentials — modal verbs + travel vocabulary
0%
▼

Vocabulary Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Estude cada palavra de aeroporto, ouça a pronúncia e leia o exemplo.

Boarding pass•cartão de embarque
The card that lets you get on the plane
"Here is my passport and boarding pass."
Gate•portão de embarque
The door where you board the plane
"Your flight departs from gate twenty-two."
Check-in•check-in
Where you register for your flight
"How many bags do you have for check-in?"
Luggage•bagagem
Your bags and suitcases
"Can I take this luggage on board?"
Departure•partida / embarque
When the plane leaves
"Departure is at three forty."
Customs•alfândega
Where officials check what you bring into a country
"You have to go through customs."
Aisle seat•assento no corredor
A seat next to the walking path on the plane
"Could I have an aisle seat, please?"
Delay•atraso
When the flight is late
"Is there any delay with the flight?"

Vocabulary Stage 1.2: Matching

Associe cada palavra em inglês com a tradução correta em português.

Boarding pass
Gate
Check-in
Luggage
Departure
Customs
Aisle seat
Delay

Grammar Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Leia o texto. Os modais estão em negrito. Depois, responda as perguntas.

Rafael is at the airport for his flight to New York. At check-in, he shows his passport and boarding pass. The agent says he must be at the gate thirty minutes before departure. His bag is big, so the agent says he should check it in.

Rafael asks, "Can I have an aisle seat?" Then he has to go through security and customs. The flight is on time, with no delay. He can't bring large liquids in his hand luggage.
1. When must Rafael be at the gate?
A Five minutes before departure
B Thirty minutes before departure
C One hour after departure
2. Why does the agent say "you should check it in"?
A His bag is big (advice)
B The flight is delayed
C He has no passport
3. What can't Rafael bring in his hand luggage?
A His passport
B Large liquids
C His boarding pass

Grammar Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip

Os modais para regras, conselho e permissão.

Modal Verbs
MODAL USO / EXEMPLO
must / have toobrigação (regra): "You must show your passport."
shouldconselho: "You should arrive early."
canpermissão: "Can I take this on board?"
can't / mustn'tproibição: "You can't bring large liquids."

✗ "You must to show your passport." (errado)

✓ "You must show your passport." (modal + verbo base, sem 'to')

Practice Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blanks

Complete as frases com o vocabulário e os modais corretos.

"Here is my passport and ."
" I take this luggage on board?"
"Could I have an seat, please?"
"I be at the gate before departure."
"Is there any with the flight?"
"You go through security."
"You check it in."

Order Stage 2: Through the Airport

Organize as frases na ordem correta de uma passagem pelo aeroporto. Ouça primeiro.

?"Could I have an aisle seat, please?"
?"I will board at gate twenty-two."
?"Good morning. Here is my passport and boarding pass."
?"I must go through security and customs."
?"I have one bag to check in."

Speaking Stage 3: Pronunciation

Ouça cada frase, grave sua voz e compare a pronúncia.

Here is my passport and boarding pass.
Can I take this luggage on board?
Could I have an aisle seat, please?
I must be at the gate before departure.
Is there any delay with the flight?

Quiz Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação no aeroporto.

The agent asks for your documents. What do you say?
A "Here is my paper."
B "Here is my passport and boarding pass."
C "I have nothing."
You want permission to take a bag on board. What do you say?
A "Can I take this luggage on board?"
B "I take this bag, yes?"
C "This bag goes with me, must."
Which sentence is correct for a rule?
A "You must to show your passport."
B "You must show your passport."
C "You must showing your passport."
Your bag is too big. What is good advice?
A "You must to leave the bag."
B "You should check it in."
C "You can't have a bag."

Reflection Stage 5: Free Production

Grave sua resposta livremente. Não há certo ou errado — o objetivo é praticar.

Record a short airport conversation out loud in English. Check in for a flight: show your documents, check a bag, ask for an aisle seat, and ask one question (about the gate, the time, or a delay). Use must, have to, should, or can.

Survival Card — Lesson 10

1Here is my passport and boarding pass.Aqui está meu passaporte e cartão de embarque.
2Can I take this luggage on board?Posso levar esta bagagem a bordo?
3Could I have an aisle seat, please?Eu poderia ter um assento no corredor, por favor?
4I must be at the gate before departure.Eu devo estar no portão antes do embarque.
5Is there any delay with the flight?Há algum atraso no voo?

Checklist — Aula 10

  • Estudei as 8 palavras de aeroporto
  • Completei o matching
  • Li o texto de Grammar in Context
  • Revisei o Grammar Tip (modais)
  • Completei os fill-in-the-blanks
  • Organizei a passagem pelo aeroporto
  • Pratiquei a pronúncia (5 frases)
  • Respondi o quiz situacional
  • Gravei a produção livre
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

Hotel & Restaurant -- Checking In and Ordering

Resolva a parte mais difícil de viajar a trabalho: faça o check-in no hotel, peça o que precisa no quarto e no restaurante, e pergunte preços e quantidades com naturalidade.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Hotel & Restaurant Vocabulary

Vocabulary

Listen to each word and read the example. These words help you handle a hotel and a restaurant on your own.

Reservation -- reserva
"I have a reservation under the name Rafael."
Front desk -- recepção (balcão do hotel)
"Please leave the key at the front desk."
Key card -- cartão-chave do quarto
"Here is your key card for room 502."
Guest -- hóspede
"Breakfast is free for all guests."
Waiter -- garçom
"The waiter brought us the menu."
Menu -- cardápio
"Could I see the menu, please?"
To order -- pedir (comida/bebida)
"I would like to order the grilled fish."
Bill -- conta (do restaurante)
"Could I have the bill, please?"
Tip -- gorjeta
"In the US, a 15% tip is normal."
Refund -- reembolso / devolução do dinheiro
"They gave me a refund for the wrong charge."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Reservation
Front desk
Key card
Guest
Waiter
Menu
To order
Bill
Tip
Refund

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

Rafael arrives at his hotel in New York. He goes to the front desk and says, "I have a reservation under Gasparelli." The guest service is fast. "How many nights are you staying?" the receptionist asks. "Three nights," he says. She gives him his key card. Later, at the restaurant, the waiter brings the menu. "Is there any grilled fish today?" Rafael asks. "Yes, and we have some fresh salad too." He decides to order the fish. At the end, he asks, "How much is the bill?" and leaves a small tip.

1. Where does Rafael show his reservation?
A At the front desk.
B At the airport gate.
C On the plane.
2. Which question asks about a countable noun (nights)?
A "How much is the bill?"
B "How many nights are you staying?"
C "Is there any fish?"
3. What does Rafael do at the end of the meal?
A He cooks the dinner.
B He asks for the bill and leaves a tip.
C He cancels the reservation.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Some / Any & How much / How many

Grammar

Para pedir coisas, você precisa saber se o substantivo é contável (countável: one room, two nights) ou incontável (uncountable: water, coffee, money). Use some em frases afirmativas e any em perguntas e negativas. Para perguntar quantidade, use How many com contáveis e How much com incontáveis. / Use some in positive sentences and any in questions and negatives. Use How many with countable nouns and How much with uncountable nouns.

Tipo / TypeEnglishPortuguês
some (+ frase)I would like some water.Eu gostaria de um pouco de água.
any (? / negativo)Is there any coffee?Tem café?
How many (contável)How many nights are you staying?Quantas noites você vai ficar?
How much (incontável)How much is the room?Quanto custa o quarto?
any (negativo)There isn't any sugar.Não tem açúcar.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"I have a under Gasparelli."
" nights are you staying?"
" is the bill?"
"Is there coffee?"
"I would like water."
"Could I have the , please?"

Stage 2: Order the Check-in

Order

Listen first, then put Rafael's evening in order.

?Next, she gives him the key card and explains the breakfast time.
?Finally, he asks for the bill and thanks the staff for the service.
?First, Rafael arrives and tells the receptionist he has a reservation.
?After that, Rafael goes to the restaurant and orders dinner from the waiter.
?Then the receptionist confirms his booking and asks for his passport.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Speak slowly and clearly.

I have a reservation under Gasparelli.
Eu tenho uma reserva no nome Gasparelli.
How many nights are you staying?
Quantas noites você vai ficar?
I would like to order some grilled fish.
Eu gostaria de pedir um peixe grelhado.
Could I have the bill, please?
Poderia me trazer a conta, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You arrive at your hotel in New York. What do you say at the front desk?
A "Hello, I have a reservation under Gasparelli."
B "Where is the airport gate?"
C "Goodbye and good night."
You want to ask the price of the room. What is correct?
A "How many is the room?"
B "How much is the room?"
C "How room is the much?"
The waiter is at your table. How do you order water politely?
A "Give me water now."
B "I would like some water, please."
C "Are you any water?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine you just arrived at your hotel in New York after a long flight. Do the full check-in out loud: greet the receptionist, give your reservation name, answer how many nights you are staying, and ask one question about the breakfast or the room. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Aula 11

1I have a reservation under Gasparelli.Eu tenho uma reserva no nome Gasparelli.
2How much is the room?Quanto custa o quarto?
3I would like to order, please.Eu gostaria de pedir, por favor.
4Is there any vegetarian food?Tem alguma comida vegetariana?
5Could I have the bill, please?Poderia me trazer a conta, por favor?
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Review & Consolidation -- Lessons 7 to 11

Junte tudo o que você aprendeu nas aulas 7 a 11 (e-mails, ligações, números e dados, aeroporto, hotel e restaurante) em uma única viagem de negócios completa, do primeiro e-mail até o jantar.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words in Review

Vocabulary

These are not new words. Listen to each one and remember how you used it in Lessons 7 to 11.

Attachment -- anexo (de e-mail)
"Please find the report in the attachment."
Agenda -- pauta / agenda da reunião
"The agenda for the call has three points."
Figure -- número / cifra
"The sales figure for the quarter is strong."
Growth -- crescimento
"We had good growth this year."
Customs -- alfândega
"I had nothing to declare at customs."
Gate -- portão de embarque
"The flight leaves from gate 22."
Key card -- cartão-chave do quarto
"Here is your key card for room 502."
Deadline -- prazo final
"The deadline for the report is Friday."
To order -- pedir (comida/bebida)
"I would like to order the grilled fish."
Bill -- conta (do restaurante)
"Could I have the bill, please?"

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Attachment
Agenda
Figure
Growth
Customs
Gate
Key card
Deadline
To order
Bill

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

Rafael prepares for a business trip. First, he writes an email with a clear subject line and sends the agenda as an attachment. He checks the figures for the quarter: the growth is better than last year. On the phone, a client speaks fast, so he says, "Could you speak more slowly, please?" At the airport, he passes customs and finds his gate. At the hotel, the clerk gives him his key card. Later, at dinner, he decides to order the fish and asks, "Is there any water?" Finally, he asks for the bill and remembers the deadline for the report on Friday.

1. How does Rafael send the agenda?
A As an attachment in an email.
B At the airport gate.
C On the hotel key card.
2. What does he say when the client speaks fast?
A "How many is the room?"
B "Could you speak more slowly, please?"
C "I would like any water."
3. How is the growth this quarter?
A The same as last year.
B Better than last year.
C Worse than last year.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Your Three Tools in Review

Grammar

Esta aula revisa as três ferramentas dos blocos 7 a 11. Peça com educação usando Can / Could you...?. Compare duas coisas com -er than (short adjectives) ou more ... than (long adjectives). E escolha some em frases afirmativas e any em perguntas e negativas; use How many com contáveis e How much com incontáveis. / Ask politely with Can / Could you. Compare with -er than or more ... than. Use some in positive sentences and any in questions and negatives; How many with countable nouns and How much with uncountable nouns.

Tool / FerramentaEnglishPortuguês
Could you...? (request)Could you repeat that, please?Você poderia repetir, por favor?
-er than (compare)Our profit is higher than last year.Nosso lucro é maior que no ano passado.
some (+ frase)I would like some water.Eu gostaria de um pouco de água.
any (? / negativo)Is there any news?Tem alguma novidade?
How much (incontável)How much is the room?Quanto custa o quarto?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

" speak more slowly, please?"
"Our profit is last year."
"I would like water."
"Is there news from New York?"
" is the room per night?"
"Could I have the , please?"

Stage 2: Order the Trip

Order

Listen first, then put Rafael's business trip in order.

?Next, he checks the figures in the quarterly report before the trip.
?Finally, he checks in at the hotel and orders dinner at the restaurant.
?First, Rafael writes an email to confirm the meeting and attaches the agenda.
?After that, he arrives at the airport, goes through customs, and finds his gate.
?Then he makes a video call and asks the client to speak more slowly.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Speak slowly and clearly.

Could you speak more slowly, please?
Você poderia falar mais devagar, por favor?
Our profit is higher than last year.
Nosso lucro é maior que no ano passado.
Please find the agenda in the attachment.
Segue a pauta em anexo.
Could I have the bill, please?
Poderia me trazer a conta, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You finish a business email and want to attach the agenda. What do you write?
A "Please find the agenda in the attachment."
B "Where is the airport gate?"
C "Give me water now."
On a video call, the speaker is too fast. What is the polite request?
A "Speak slowly now."
B "Could you speak more slowly, please?"
C "How much you speak?"
You want to compare this quarter's profit with last year. What is correct?
A "Our profit is more better."
B "Our profit is higher than last year."
C "Our profit is the higher."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Imagine your next business trip from start to end. Out loud, describe the whole journey in English: write the first email and its subject line, open the video call, explain one figure from the report, pass the airport, check in at the hotel, and order dinner. Take your time and use the tools from Lessons 7 to 11.

Survival Card -- Aula 12

1Please find the agenda in the attachment.Segue a pauta em anexo.
2Could you speak more slowly, please?Você poderia falar mais devagar, por favor?
3Our profit is higher than last year.Nosso lucro é maior que no ano passado.
4I have a reservation under Gasparelli.Eu tenho uma reserva no nome Gasparelli.
5Could I have the bill, please?Poderia me trazer a conta, por favor?
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

What Happened? -- Describing Past Events

Aprenda a contar o que sua empresa fez no passado. Oito verbos de ação (lançar, assinar, contratar, entregar...) e o Past Simple dos verbos regulares: a terminação -ed, a negativa com did not e a pergunta com Did.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words

Vocabulary

Eight action verbs to tell a business story. Listen to each one and notice the past form with -ed.

Launch -- lançar (produto/serviço)
"We launched a new service in January."
Sign -- assinar (um contrato)
"The board signed the contract in February."
Hire -- contratar (alguém)
"We hired four new lawyers."
Deliver -- entregar (mercadoria/relatório)
"We delivered all the reports on time."
Finish -- terminar / concluir
"We finished every project on time."
Improve -- melhorar
"The numbers improved month after month."
Open -- abrir (escritório/loja)
"We opened a small office in Rio."
Achieve -- alcançar (uma meta)
"We achieved our main goal for the quarter."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each verb with its meaning in Portuguese.

Launch
Sign
Hire
Deliver
Finish
Improve
Open
Achieve

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

Last quarter was a strong one for Rafael's company. In January, they launched a new service for corporate clients. In February, the board signed an important contract, and the team hired four new lawyers. They also opened a small office in Rio. The numbers improved every month. By March, they finished every project and delivered all the reports on time. They did not miss a single deadline, and in the end they achieved their main goal.
