1. What did the company launch in January?
A A new service for corporate clients.
B A small office in Rio.
C Four new lawyers.
2. What did the board do in February?
A It finished every project.
B It signed an important contract.
C It delivered the reports.
3. Did they miss any deadline?
A Yes, they missed two deadlines.
B No, they did not miss a single deadline.
C The story does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Past Simple (Regular Verbs)

Grammar

Para falar do passado com verbos regulares, acrescente -ed ao verbo: launch → launched, sign → signed. Na negativa, use did not (didn't) + verbo na forma base (sem -ed): "We did not finish." Na pergunta, use Did + sujeito + verbo base: "Did you deliver the report?". Atenção: depois de did/did not, o verbo SEMPRE volta à forma base. / For the past with regular verbs, add -ed. For negatives, use did not + base verb. For questions, use Did + subject + base verb. After did or did not, the verb always returns to its base form.

Form / FormaEnglishPortuguês
Affirmative (verb + -ed)We launched a new service.Nós lançamos um novo serviço.
Affirmative (verb + -ed)The board signed the contract.A diretoria assinou o contrato.
Negative (did not + base)We did not finish the audit.Nós não terminamos a auditoria.
Question (Did + base)Did you deliver the report?Você entregou o relatório?
Short answerYes, we did. / No, we did not.Sim, entregamos. / Não, não entregamos.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"Last quarter, we a new service."
"The board the contract in February."
"We did not the audit last week."
" you deliver the report on time?"
"The numbers every month."
"We our main goal for the quarter."

Stage 2: Order the Quarter

Order

Listen first, then put the company's quarter in order.

?Next, the team hired four new lawyers and opened a small office in Rio.
?Finally, the company achieved its main goal for the quarter.
?First, in January, the company launched a new service for corporate clients.
?After that, they finished every project on time and delivered all the reports.
?Then, in February, the board signed an important contract with a partner in New York.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Pronounce the -ed ending clearly.

We launched a new service in January.
Nós lançamos um novo serviço em janeiro.
The board signed an important contract.
A diretoria assinou um contrato importante.
We finished every project and delivered the reports.
Nós terminamos todos os projetos e entregamos os relatórios.
Did you deliver the report on time?
Você entregou o relatório no prazo?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to say the company started a new product last month. What is correct?
A "We launched a new product last month."
B "We launch a new product last month."
C "We did launched a new product."
You want to ask a colleague if the team finished the project. What do you say?
A "Did the team finished the project?"
B "Did the team finish the project?"
C "The team finish the project?"
You want to say you did not reach the goal. What is correct?
A "We not achieved the goal."
B "We did not achieve the goal."
C "We did not achieved the goal."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about your company or your team last quarter. Out loud, in English, describe what happened: what you launched, what you signed, who you hired, what you finished and delivered, and what goal you achieved. Use the past with -ed, and remember the base verb after did and did not.

Survival Card -- Aula 13

1We launched a new service last quarter.Nós lançamos um novo serviço no trimestre passado.
2The board signed an important contract.A diretoria assinou um contrato importante.
3We finished and delivered all the reports.Nós terminamos e entregamos todos os relatórios.
4Did you deliver the report on time?Você entregou o relatório no prazo?
5We achieved our main goal for the quarter.Nós alcançamos nossa principal meta do trimestre.
Aula 14 -- Pre-class

Company History -- Milestones and Changes

Aprenda a contar a história de uma empresa no passado. Oito verbos de marcos (fundar, crescer, construir, comprar, vencer...) e o Past Simple dos verbos IRREGULARES: formas que mudam (grow → grew, become → became), a negativa com did not e a pergunta com Did.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words

Vocabulary

Eight milestone verbs to tell a company's history. Listen to each one and notice the irregular past form.

Found -- fundar (passado: founded)
"Two lawyers founded the firm in 1998."
Grow -- crescer (passado: grew)
"The firm grew slowly in the first years."
Become -- tornar-se (passado: became)
"It became one of the most respected firms."
Build -- construir / desenvolver (passado: built)
"The firm built a strong reputation."
Buy -- comprar (passado: bought)
"In 2005, it bought a new office."
Win -- vencer / ganhar (passado: won)
"The firm won a national award in 2012."
Take over -- assumir o controle (passado: took over)
"It took over two local competitors."
Merge -- fundir / juntar (subst.: merger = fusão)
"After a big merger, it opened offices abroad."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each verb with its meaning in Portuguese.

Found
Grow
Become
Build
Buy
Win
Take over
Merge

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story and answer the questions.

In 1998, two lawyers founded a small firm with a single desk. In the first years, the firm grew slowly, but it built a strong reputation. In 2005, it bought a new office and took over two local competitors. The firm became one of the most respected in the region, and in 2012 it won a national award. After a big merger, the company opened offices in three countries. They did not stop, and they never forgot how it all began.

1. Who founded the firm in 1998?
A Two lawyers, with a single desk.
B Two local competitors.
C A national award committee.
2. What did the firm do in 2005?
A It founded a new firm.
B It bought a new office and took over two competitors.
C It won a national award.
3. What happened after the big merger?
A The firm closed two offices.
B The company opened offices in three countries.
C The story does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Past Simple (Irregular Verbs)

Grammar

Muitos verbos comuns NÃO seguem a regra do -ed: eles têm uma forma própria no passado que você precisa memorizar. Ex: grow → grew, become → became, build → built, buy → bought, win → won. Na negativa e na pergunta, porém, vale a mesma regra dos regulares: use did not / Did + o verbo na forma BASE (sem mudança): "We did not win" / "Did the firm grow?". / Many common verbs do not follow the -ed rule: they have a special past form you must memorize. But for negatives and questions, use did not / Did + the base verb, just like regular verbs.

Form / FormaEnglishPortuguês
Affirmative (irregular form)The firm grew quickly.A firma cresceu rapidamente.
Affirmative (irregular form)It became a market leader.Ela se tornou líder de mercado.
Negative (did not + base)We did not win the first time.Nós não vencemos na primeira vez.
Question (Did + base)Did the firm buy a new office?A firma comprou um novo escritório?
Short answerYes, it did. / No, it did not.Sim, comprou. / Não, não comprou.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct irregular form. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"In 1998, two lawyers the firm."
"The firm slowly in the first years."
"It a strong reputation in the city."
" the company buy a new office?"
"In 2012, the firm a national award."
"After the merger, it a market leader."

Stage 2: Order the History

Order

Listen first, then put the company's history in order.

?Next, in 2005, it bought a new office and took over two local competitors.
?Finally, after a big merger, the company opened offices in three countries.
?First, in 1998, two lawyers founded the firm with a single desk.
?After that, the firm became one of the most respected and won a national award.
?Then, in the first years, the firm grew slowly and built a strong reputation.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Pronounce the irregular past forms clearly.

Two lawyers founded the firm in 1998.
Dois advogados fundaram a firma em 1998.
The firm grew and built a strong reputation.
A firma cresceu e construiu uma forte reputação.
It bought an office and won a national award.
Ela comprou um escritório e venceu um prêmio nacional.
Did the firm grow after the merger?
A firma cresceu depois da fusão?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to say the company got bigger every year. What is correct?
A "The company growed every year."
B "The company grew every year."
C "The company did grew every year."
You want to ask if the firm bought a new office. What do you say?
A "Did the firm bought a new office?"
B "Did the firm buy a new office?"
C "The firm buy a new office?"
You want to say the firm did not win the first time. What is correct?
A "The firm not won the first time."
B "The firm did not win the first time."
C "The firm did not won the first time."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about your company, or a company you admire. Out loud, in English, tell its history: who founded it, how it grew, what it built, what it bought, which firms it took over or merged with, and what it became today. Use the irregular past forms, and remember the base verb after did and did not.

Survival Card -- Aula 14

1Two lawyers founded the firm in 1998.Dois advogados fundaram a firma em 1998.
2The firm grew and built a strong reputation.A firma cresceu e construiu uma forte reputação.
3It bought an office and took over two firms.Ela comprou um escritório e assumiu duas firmas.
4In 2012, it won a national award.Em 2012, ela venceu um prêmio nacional.
5After the merger, it became a market leader.Depois da fusão, ela se tornou líder de mercado.
Aula 15 -- Pre-class

Up to Now -- Present Perfect

Aprenda a conectar o passado ao presente: o que já aconteceu ATÉ AGORA. Oito palavras de tempo (already, just, yet, ever, never, so far, recently, lately) e o Present Perfect: have/has + participio, a negativa com have/has not e a pergunta com Have/Has. Use para resultados que ainda contam neste ano.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words

Vocabulary

Eight time words that signal the Present Perfect. Listen to each one and notice where it goes in the sentence.

Already -- já (antes do esperado)
"We have already signed three clients."
Just -- acabou de / há pouco
"We have just opened a new office."
Yet -- ainda (perguntas e negativas)
"We have not finished the program yet."
Ever -- alguma vez (na vida, até agora)
"Have you ever worked abroad?"
Never -- nunca (nenhuma vez)
"I have never stopped studying."
So far -- até agora / até o momento
"So far, revenue has grown a lot."
Recently -- recentemente / há pouco tempo
"We have recently hired two managers."
Lately -- ultimamente / nos últimos dias
"The team has worked very hard lately."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each time word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Already
Just
Yet
Ever
Never
So far
Recently
Lately

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the update and answer the questions.

This year has been strong so far. We have already signed three major clients, and we have just opened a new office in the south. Revenue has grown by twelve percent since January, and we have recently hired two new managers. The team has worked very hard lately. We have not finished the new training program yet, but it is almost ready. We have never had such a positive start to a year.

1. How many clients has the firm signed so far this year?
A Three major clients.
B Two new managers.
C Twelve clients.
2. What has the firm not done yet?
A It has not opened a new office.
B It has not finished the new training program.
C It has not hired any managers.
3. How much has revenue grown since January?
A By three percent.
B By twelve percent.
C The text does not say.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Perfect

Grammar

O Present Perfect liga o passado ao presente: usamos para ações de um tempo que ainda não terminou (this year, so far) ou cujo resultado ainda conta agora. A estrutura é have/has + participio (have signed, has opened). Use have com I/you/we/they e has com he/she/it. Na negativa: have/has not + participio. Na pergunta: Have/Has + sujeito + participio. Para um tempo terminado (yesterday, in 2005) use o Past Simple, não o Present Perfect. / The Present Perfect connects past and present. Use have/has + past participle for time not finished yet or results that still matter now.

Form / FormaEnglishPortuguês
Affirmative (have)We have already signed three clients.Nós já assinamos três clientes.
Affirmative (has)Revenue has grown since January.A receita cresceu desde janeiro.
Negative (have/has not)We have not finished the program yet.Não terminamos o programa ainda.
Question (Have/Has)Have you ever worked abroad?Você já trabalhou no exterior alguma vez?
Short answerYes, I have. / No, I have not.Sim, já. / Não, ainda não.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct Present Perfect form. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"We already signed three major clients."
"The firm has just a new office."
"Revenue grown since January."
"We have not the program yet."
" you ever worked abroad?"
"The team has worked very hard ."

Stage 2: Order the Year

Order

Listen first, then put the year update in order.

?Next, revenue has grown by twelve percent since January.
?Finally, the company has not finished the program yet, but it is almost ready.
?First, the company has already signed three major clients this year.
?After that, the team has worked hard on a new training program.
?Then, it has just opened a new office in the south.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Pronounce have and has clearly before the participle.

We have already signed three major clients.
Nós já assinamos três grandes clientes.
The firm has just opened a new office.
A firma acabou de abrir um novo escritório.
We have not finished the program yet.
Nós não terminamos o programa ainda.
Have you ever worked abroad?
Você já trabalhou no exterior alguma vez?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to say the firm has signed a client (result connected to now). What is correct?
A "The firm have signed a new client."
B "The firm has signed a new client."
C "The firm has sign a new client."
You want to ask a colleague about their life experience. What do you say?
A "Have you ever led a big team?"
B "Do you ever led a big team?"
C "Have you ever leading a big team?"
The report is still open. How do you say it is not done?
A "We have finished the report yet."
B "We have not finished the report yet."
C "We have not finished the report yesterday."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about this year at your company, or about your own career. Out loud, in English, report what you or your company has achieved so far, and what you have not done yet. Use have/has + participle with already, just, yet, so far, recently, and never.

Survival Card -- Aula 15

1We have already signed three major clients.Nós já assinamos três grandes clientes.
2The firm has just opened a new office.A firma acabou de abrir um novo escritório.
3Revenue has grown since January.A receita cresceu desde janeiro.
4We have not finished the program yet.Nós não terminamos o programa ainda.
5Have you ever worked abroad?Você já trabalhou no exterior alguma vez?
Aula 16 -- Pre-class

At the Board Table -- Opinions and Decisions

Aprenda a falar em uma reunião de diretoria: dar a sua opinião (I think / In my opinion), concordar e discordar com respeito (I agree / I disagree, nunca "I am agree") e chegar a uma decisão. Vocabulário de decisão: proposal, vote, approve, reject, concern, reasonable, compromise, point of view.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words

Vocabulary

Eight words you hear in every board meeting. Listen to each one and notice how it is used.

Proposal -- proposta (ideia para discutir)
"The proposal is to open a second office."
Vote -- voto / votar
"Let us take a vote on the proposal."
Approve -- aprovar (dizer sim)
"The board has approved the new budget."
Reject -- rejeitar (dizer não)
"They rejected the first proposal."
Concern -- preocupação / receio
"I understand the concern about the cost."
Reasonable -- razoável / sensato
"That is a reasonable price for the office."
Compromise -- acordo / meio-termo
"We found a reasonable compromise."
Point of view -- ponto de vista / opinião
"Each director shared a point of view."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each board word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Proposal
Vote
Approve
Reject
Concern
Reasonable
Compromise
Point of view

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the board minutes and answer the questions.

At the board meeting, the chair presented a proposal to open a second office in the south. In my opinion, this is the right moment, she said. One director said, I agree, the new clients there need a local office. But another director had a concern about the cost. I see your point, but I disagree, he said. I think the cost is too high this year. After a long discussion, the board found a reasonable compromise: start with a small office. Actually, that is a smart idea, the chair said. Then the directors took a vote, and the majority approved the new plan.

1. What is the proposal at the meeting?
A To open a second office in the south.
B To hire two new managers.
C To close the main office.
2. Why does one director disagree?
A The location is bad.
B He thinks the cost is too high this year.
C There are no clients in the south.
3. What compromise does the board reach?
A They reject the proposal.
B They start with a small office.
C They wait until next year.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Opinions, Agree & Disagree

Grammar

Para dar uma opinião, comece com I think ou In my opinion seguido de uma frase completa (sujeito + verbo): "In my opinion, it is a good plan." Para concordar, diga I agree — NUNCA "I am agree" (agree já é verbo). Para discordar, diga I disagree ou, com mais respeito, I see your point, but... Use Actually para corrigir uma ideia com gentileza. / To give an opinion use I think / In my opinion + full sentence. To agree say I agree (never "I am agree"). To disagree politely use I see your point, but...

Function / FunçãoEnglishPortuguês
Give an opinionIn my opinion, it is a good plan.Na minha opinião, é um bom plano.
Give an opinionI think we should wait.Eu acho que deveríamos esperar.
AgreeI agree with you.Eu concordo com você.
Disagree (polite)I see your point, but I disagree.Entendo seu ponto, mas discordo.
Correct gentlyActually, the cost is lower.Na verdade, o custo é menor.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct opinion or decision word. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"In my , this is the right moment."
"I with you completely."
"I see your point, but I ."
"I understand the about the cost."
"Let us find a reasonable ."
"Let us take a on the proposal."

Stage 2: Order the Meeting

Order

Listen first, then put the board meeting in order.

?Next, the board discusses the main concern, which is the cost.
?Finally, the board votes, and the majority approves the new plan.
?First, the chair opens the meeting and presents the proposal.
?After that, the directors look for a reasonable compromise.
?Then, each director shares a point of view about the idea.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each phrase, then record yourself saying it. Speak your opinion clearly and with confidence.

In my opinion, this is the right moment.
Na minha opinião, este é o momento certo.
I agree with you completely.
Eu concordo totalmente com você.
I see your point, but I disagree.
Entendo seu ponto, mas discordo.
Let us find a reasonable compromise.
Vamos encontrar um acordo razoável.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best response for each situation.

You want to agree with a colleague's idea. What is correct?
A "I am agree with you."
B "I agree with you."
C "I am agreeing you."
You want to give your opinion that the plan is good. What do you say?
A "In my opinion, it is a good plan."
B "In my opinion is a good plan."
C "For my opinion a good plan."
You disagree, but you want to be respectful. What do you say?
A "You are completely wrong."
B "I see your point, but I disagree."
C "I am disagree with that."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about a real decision at your company. Out loud, in English, give your opinion about it with one reason, and say where you agree and where you disagree. Use I think, In my opinion, I agree, I disagree, and I see your point, but...

Survival Card -- Aula 16

1In my opinion, this is the right moment.Na minha opinião, este é o momento certo.
2I agree with you completely.Eu concordo totalmente com você.
3I see your point, but I disagree.Entendo seu ponto, mas discordo.
4Let us find a reasonable compromise.Vamos encontrar um acordo razoável.
5Let us take a vote on the proposal.Vamos votar a proposta.
Aula 17 -- Pre-class

Legal Basics -- Contracts and Terms

Aprenda as palavras-chave de um contrato (contract, clause, party, obligation, breach, agreement, binding, terminate) e os modais juridicos: shall e must para dever, may para permissao e must not para o que e proibido. Sempre modal + verbo na base (must pay, shall deliver), sem "to" e sem "-s".
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words

Vocabulary

Eight words that appear in almost every contract. Listen to each one and notice how it is used.

Contract -- contrato (acordo legal por escrito)
"Both sides signed the contract today."
Agreement -- acordo (promessa que ambas as partes aceitam)
"The agreement is valid for two years."
Clause -- cláusula (uma regra/parte do contrato)
"Clause five is about payment."
Party -- parte (pessoa ou empresa que assina)
"Each party must follow the rules."
Obligation -- obrigação / dever
"Payment is an obligation of the client."
Breach -- quebra / violação de uma cláusula
"A late payment is a breach of contract."
Binding -- vinculante (de cumprimento obrigatório por lei)
"A signed contract is binding."
Terminate -- encerrar / rescindir um contrato
"Either party may terminate the agreement."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each legal word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Contract
Agreement
Clause
Party
Obligation
Breach
Binding
Terminate

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the contract summary and answer the questions.

This agreement is valid for two years. The supplier shall deliver the goods within thirty days of each order. The client must pay each invoice within fifteen days. Neither party may share confidential information with a third company; in other words, the parties must not share the data. A signed contract is binding, so both parties must respect every clause. If one party breaks a clause, that is a breach of contract, and the other party may terminate the agreement with a written notice.

1. What must the client do within fifteen days?
A Pay each invoice.
B Deliver the goods.
C Terminate the agreement.
2. What does "may terminate" mean here?
A The party has to end the contract.
B The party is allowed to end the contract.
C The party cannot end the contract.
3. What is forbidden under this agreement?
A Paying an invoice.
B Delivering the goods.
C Sharing confidential information with a third company.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Shall, Must, May

Grammar

Em contratos, usamos modais para dizer o que cada parte deve, pode ou não pode fazer. Shall e must indicam um dever/obrigação (the supplier shall deliver, the client must pay). May indica permissão, não obrigação (either party may terminate). Must not indica algo proibido (the parties must not share the data). Depois do modal, sempre use o verbo na forma base (deliver, pay, share), nunca "to" e nunca "-s". / In contracts, shall/must = duty, may = permission, must not = forbidden. After the modal, always use the base verb.

ModalEnglishPortuguês
shall (dever, legal)The supplier shall deliver the goods.O fornecedor deverá entregar as mercadorias.
must (obrigação)The client must pay each invoice.O cliente deve pagar cada fatura.
may (permissão)Either party may terminate the agreement.Qualquer parte pode rescindir o acordo.
must not (proibido)The parties must not share the data.As partes não podem compartilhar os dados.
Regra de ouromodal + base verb (no "to", no "-s")modal + verbo na base (sem "to", sem "-s")

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each clause with the correct modal or verb. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"The supplier deliver the goods within thirty days."
"The client must each invoice within fifteen days."
"Either party terminate the agreement."
"The parties must not confidential information."
"A signed contract is ."
"A late payment is a of contract."

Stage 2: Order the Signing

Order

Listen first, then put the signing steps in order.

?Next, each party checks that the agreement is valid and fair.
?Finally, if one party breaks a clause, the other party may terminate the agreement.
?First, both parties read every clause of the contract carefully.
?After that, both parties sign the contract on the signing date.
?Then, the lawyer explains the main obligations and terms.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each clause, then record yourself saying it. Say the modal and the base verb clearly.

The supplier shall deliver the goods within thirty days.
O fornecedor deverá entregar as mercadorias em trinta dias.
The client must pay each invoice within fifteen days.
O cliente deve pagar cada fatura em quinze dias.
Either party may terminate the agreement.
Qualquer parte pode rescindir o acordo.
The parties must not share confidential information.
As partes não podem compartilhar informações confidenciais.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best clause for each situation.

You want to write that the client has a duty to pay. What is correct?
A "The client must to pay the invoice."
B "The client must pay the invoice."
C "The client must pays the invoice."
You want to give a party permission to end the contract. What do you write?
A "Either party may terminate the agreement."
B "Either party must terminate the agreement."
C "Either party may terminates the agreement."
Sharing the data is forbidden. How do you write that rule?
A "The parties may share the data."
B "The parties must not share the data."
C "The parties not must share the data."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about a contract you know from your work. Out loud, in English, explain three of its rules: one duty with shall or must, one permission with may, and one thing that is forbidden with must not. Use the words clause, party, and obligation.

Survival Card -- Aula 17

1Both parties signed the contract.Ambas as partes assinaram o contrato.
2The supplier shall deliver the goods within thirty days.O fornecedor deverá entregar as mercadorias em trinta dias.
3The client must pay each invoice.O cliente deve pagar cada fatura.
4Either party may terminate the agreement.Qualquer parte pode rescindir o acordo.
5The parties must not share confidential information.As partes não podem compartilhar informações confidenciais.
Aula 18 -- Pre-class

Negotiating the Deal -- Offers and Counter-offers

Aprenda as palavras-chave de uma negociação (offer, counter-offer, discount, quote, bargain, installment, deposit, flexible) e o first conditional para fazer ofertas: if + presente simples na condição e will + verbo na base no resultado -- "If you sign this month, we will give you a discount". Nunca use "will" depois de "if".
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words

Vocabulary

Eight words you hear in almost every negotiation. Listen to each one and notice how it is used.

Offer -- oferta (preço ou condição que você propõe)
"Our first offer is two hundred thousand."
Counter-offer -- contraproposta (resposta com preço diferente)
"We sent them our counter-offer."
Discount -- desconto (dinheiro tirado do preço)
"They gave us a five percent discount."
Quote -- orçamento (preço proposto por escrito)
"Please send me a quote for the project."
Bargain -- pechinchar / negociar o preço
"Let us bargain a little on the price."
Installment -- parcela (um de vários pagamentos)
"We will pay in three installments."
Deposit -- sinal / entrada (pagamento inicial para garantir)
"They asked for a ten percent deposit."
Flexible -- flexível (aberto a mudar condições)
"We are flexible on the payment terms."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each negotiation word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Offer
Counter-offer
Discount
Quote
Bargain
Installment
Deposit
Flexible

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the negotiation summary and answer the questions.

The seller sends a quote of two hundred thousand. If the buyer signs this month, the seller will give a five percent discount. If the buyer pays in advance, the seller will offer two percent more off. The buyer answers with a counter-offer and asks to pay in installments. The seller is flexible: if the buyer pays a deposit today, the seller will accept three installments, but the discount will be smaller. If both sides agree, they will close the deal and shake hands.

1. What will the seller give if the buyer signs this month?
A A five percent discount.
B A bigger price.
C Free installments.
2. In "if the buyer pays in advance, the seller will offer more off", which tense comes after "if"?
A will + verb (future).
B The present simple (pays).
C The past simple (paid).
3. What happens to the discount if the buyer pays in installments?
A It will be bigger.
B It will be smaller.
C There will be no discount.

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The First Conditional

Grammar

Em negociações, usamos o first conditional para fazer ofertas sobre o futuro: uma condição possível e o seu resultado. Use if + presente simples na condição (if you sign, if you pay) e will + verbo na base no resultado (we will give, we will offer). Nunca use "will" depois de "if" (errado: "if you will sign"). As duas metades podem trocar de ordem: "If you sign, we will give..." = "We will give... if you sign". / In negotiations, use the first conditional: if + present simple for the condition, will + base verb for the result. Never use "will" after "if".

FormEnglishPortuguês
Condição (if + presente)If you sign this month,Se você assinar este mês,
Resultado (will + base)we will give you a discount.nós daremos um desconto.
Ordem trocadaWe will give a discount if you sign.Daremos um desconto se você assinar.
ErradoIf you will sign, we give...(nunca "will" depois de "if")
Regra de ouroif + present, ... will + base verbif + presente, ... will + verbo base

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each offer with the correct form. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

"If you this month, we will give you a discount."
"If you pay in advance, we two percent more."
"If your counter-offer reasonable, we will close the deal."
"If you pay a deposit today, we three installments."
"We will reduce the price if you more units."
"They asked for a ten percent ."

Stage 2: Order the Deal

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of a negotiation in order.

?Next, both sides meet at the table and discuss the terms.
?Finally, when both sides agree, they shake hands and close the deal.
?First, the seller sends a quote with the first price.
?After that, they bargain: if one side gives a discount, the other side offers something in return.
?Then, the buyer studies the offer and prepares a counter-offer.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each offer, then record yourself saying it. Say the "if" half and the "will" half clearly.

If you sign this month, we will give you a discount.
Se você assinar este mês, daremos um desconto.
If you pay in advance, we will offer two percent more.
Se você pagar adiantado, ofereceremos dois por cento a mais.
Our counter-offer is one hundred and seventy thousand.
Nossa contraproposta é cento e setenta mil.
We are flexible on the payment terms.
Somos flexíveis quanto às condições de pagamento.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You want to offer a discount on the condition that the client signs today. What is correct?
A "If you will sign today, we give a discount."
B "If you sign today, we will give a discount."
C "If you sign today, we giving a discount."
The client's price is too low. How do you answer politely?
A "That is below our cost, but if you pay in advance, we will accept it."
B "No. The price is the price."
C "If you will pay in advance, we accept it."
The client asks to pay in installments. You can be flexible. What do you say?
A "If you will pay a deposit, we accept installments."
B "If you pay a deposit today, we will accept three installments."
C "We accepting installments if you pay a deposit."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about a real purchase you want to negotiate (a car, a service, equipment for your firm). Out loud, in English, make three conditional offers with if + present, will + verb. Use the words offer, discount, and installment.

Survival Card -- Aula 18

1This is our first offer.Esta é a nossa primeira oferta.
2If you sign this month, we will give you a discount.Se assinar este mês, daremos um desconto.
3Can you give us a discount?Você pode nos dar um desconto?
4We are flexible on the payment terms.Somos flexíveis quanto ao pagamento.
5If we agree on the price, we have a deal.Se concordarmos no preço, temos um acordo.
Aula 19 -- Pre-class

Closing the Deal -- Finalizing and Reported Speech

Aprenda as palavras-chave de fechar um negócio (finalize, confirm, witness, effective date, enforce, valid, mutual, proceed) e o reported speech para relatar o que alguém disse: said (sem pessoa) e told + pessoa, com o verbo recuando um passo (is → was, will → would) -- "He said the price was final".
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words

Vocabulary

Eight words you hear when a deal is closing. Listen to each one and notice how it is used.

Finalize -- finalizar (deixar pronto para assinar)
"We are ready to finalize the agreement."
Confirm -- confirmar (dizer oficialmente que aceita)
"Please confirm the terms in writing."
Witness -- testemunha (quem confirma a assinatura)
"We need two witnesses for the signing."
Effective date -- data de início (dia em que o contrato começa a valer)
"The effective date is the first of next month."
Enforce -- fazer cumprir (obrigar a seguir as regras)
"A court can enforce a signed contract."
Valid -- válido (legalmente bom e em vigor)
"The contract is valid for two years."
Mutual -- mútuo (compartilhado e aceito pelos dois lados)
"This is a mutual agreement we both want."
Proceed -- prosseguir (continuar, seguir em frente)
"If you confirm, we will proceed with the signing."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each closing word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Finalize
Confirm
Witness
Enforce
Valid
Mutual
Proceed

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the report of a closed deal and answer the questions.

At the meeting, the client said the price was too high. Our director told them the quality was worth it. The client said they needed more time, and we said the offer would expire at the end of the month. A week later, the client told us they accepted the terms and said they would sign on Monday. On Monday, both sides finalized the agreement. Once it was signed, the contract became binding.

1. How do we report "The price is too high"?
A The client said the price was too high.
B The client said me the price is too high.
C The client told the price is too high.
2. In "they said they would sign on Monday", what was the direct sentence?
A "We signed on Monday."
B "We will sign on Monday."
C "We would sign on Monday."
3. Which verb takes a person directly after it (told me, told us)?
A said
B told
C both the same way

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Reported Speech

Grammar

Para relatar o que alguém disse, use said (sem pessoa depois: he said) ou told + pessoa (he told me, she told us). O verbo recua um passo para o passado: is → was, will → would, need → needed. Nunca diga "he said me" (errado) -- é "he told me" ou "he said". Nunca use "told" sem uma pessoa depois. / To report speech, use "said" (no person) or "told" + a person, and move the verb one step into the past: is to was, will to would.

Direct wordsReportedPortuguês
"The price is final."He said the price was final.Ele disse que o preço era final.
"I will sign Monday."She said she would sign on Monday.Ela disse que assinaria na segunda.
"We need more time."They told us they needed time.Eles nos disseram que precisavam de tempo.
ErradoHe said me / She told that...(use "told me" ou "said", nunca "said me")
Regra de ourosaid (no person) / told + person; is→was, will→wouldsaid sem pessoa / told + pessoa; recua o verbo

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each report with the correct form. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

Direct: "The price is final." → He said the price final.
Direct: "I will sign on Monday." → She said she sign on Monday.
Direct: "We accept the terms." → They us they accepted the terms.
Direct: "The contract is binding." → She the contract was binding.
Direct: "I need more time." → He said he more time.
"We are ready to the agreement."

Stage 2: Order the Closing

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of closing a deal in order.

?Next, they set the effective date and invite the witnesses.
?Finally, the agreement becomes binding, and both companies must follow it.
?First, both parties review the final version of the agreement.
?After that, each side signs the document and keeps one copy.
?Then, they confirm in writing that they accept all the terms.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each sentence, then record yourself saying it. Speak slowly and clearly.

We are ready to finalize the agreement.
Estamos prontos para finalizar o acordo.
He said the price was final.
Ele disse que o preço era final.
She said she would sign on Monday.
Ela disse que assinaria na segunda.
Once signed, the agreement is binding.
Uma vez assinado, o acordo é vinculante.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You report to your team what the client said: "The terms are mutual." What is correct?
A "The client said me the terms are mutual."
B "The client said the terms were mutual."
C "The client told the terms are mutual."
The partner promised: "We will confirm in writing." How do you report it to your boss?
A "The partner told me they would confirm in writing."
B "The partner said me they will confirm in writing."
C "The partner told that they will confirm in writing."
You want to close the deal politely. What do you say?
A "Sign now or we cancel."
B "Please confirm in writing, and we will proceed with the signing."
C "We proceeding if you confirm."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Think about a real business conversation you had recently. Out loud, in English, report three things people said using "said" and "told" with the verb moved back (is becomes was, will becomes would). Use the words finalize and confirm.

Survival Card -- Aula 19

1We are ready to finalize the agreement.Estamos prontos para finalizar o acordo.
2Please confirm in writing.Por favor, confirme por escrito.
3Once signed, the agreement is binding.Uma vez assinado, o acordo é vinculante.
4He said the price was final.Ele disse que o preço era final.
5They told me they would sign on Monday.Eles me disseram que assinariam na segunda.
Aula 20 -- Pre-class

Putting It All Together -- Review & Real-World Practice

Aprenda as palavras de gerenciar um negócio inteiro (stakeholder, milestone, scope, timeline, deliverable, accountable, follow-through, commitment) e revise as cinco estruturas do arco em um só lugar: present perfect, opiniões, modais must/may, primeira condicional (if + will) e reported speech (said/told) -- para conduzir um deal de ponta a ponta.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Key Words

Vocabulary

Eight words a director uses to manage a whole deal. Listen to each one and notice how it is used.

Stakeholder -- parte interessada (pessoa ou grupo com interesse no negócio)
"Every stakeholder must approve the final terms."
Milestone -- marco (etapa importante de um projeto)
"Signing the contract was a big milestone."
Scope -- escopo (todo o trabalho que o projeto inclui)
"The scope of the deal covers two countries."
Timeline -- cronograma (plano de datas do início ao fim)
"Our timeline puts the signing in March."
Deliverable -- entregável (resultado concreto que você deve entregar)
"The first deliverable is the signed contract."
Accountable -- responsável (deve responder pelo resultado)
"Each director is accountable for one milestone."
Follow-through -- cumprimento (fazer o que foi prometido depois do acordo)
"Good follow-through builds a strong partnership."
Commitment -- compromisso (promessa séria de fazer algo)
"We made a clear commitment to deliver on time."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Match each deal-management word with its meaning in Portuguese.

Stakeholder
Milestone
Scope
Timeline
Deliverable
Accountable
Commitment

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

Grammar

Read the story of a full deal. Notice the five grammar tools of the arc, then answer the questions.

This year, our team has reached two important milestones. At the first meeting, the client said the scope was too wide. In my opinion, that concern was fair. We told them that each stakeholder must approve the plan and may request small changes. Then we made a simple offer: if they accepted the timeline, we will start the next week. The client told us they would sign, and our commitment and good follow-through closed the deal.

1. How does the team talk about results up to now?
A "has reached two milestones" (present perfect)
B "reach two milestones" (present simple)
C "will reach two milestones" (future)
2. In the conditional offer, what comes after "if"?
A "if they will accept"
B "if they accepted / accept" (present, not will)
C "if they would accept"
3. How is "We will sign" reported in the text?
A "They said me they will sign."
B "They told us they would sign."
C "They told that they will sign."

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- The Five Tools Together

Grammar

Num negócio real, você mistura as cinco estruturas: present perfect para resultados (we have signed), opiniões para se posicionar (in my opinion), must/may para regras (must approve / may request), primeira condicional para ofertas (if you confirm, we will proceed -- "if" com presente, nunca "if you will") e reported speech para relatar (said sem pessoa, told + pessoa; is→was, will→would). / In a real deal you mix all five: present perfect for results, opinions to take a position, must/may for rules, if + will for offers, and said/told to report.

ToolExamplePortuguês
Present perfectWe have reached a milestone.Nós atingimos um marco.
OpinionIn my opinion, the timeline is realistic.Na minha opinião, o cronograma é realista.
Modals (must/may)Each stakeholder must approve the scope.Cada parte interessada deve aprovar o escopo.
First conditionalIf you confirm, we will proceed.Se você confirmar, nós prosseguiremos.
Reported speechHe said the deal was a milestone.Ele disse que o negócio era um marco.

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete each sentence with the correct form. Tap Ouvir to hear the full sentence.

Present perfect: We reached an important milestone.
First conditional: If you accept the scope, we finalize today.
Modal of duty: Each stakeholder approve the timeline.
Reported speech: She us she was accountable for the deliverable.
Reported speech: He said the commitment clear.
"The first is the signed contract."

Stage 2: Order the Deal

Order

Listen first, then put the steps of running a full deal in order.

?Next, one director makes a conditional offer: if you accept, we will start next week.
?Finally, the team reports the deal and keeps good follow-through on every deliverable.
?First, the team presents the scope and the first milestone to every stakeholder.
?After that, both sides confirm the commitment and sign the agreement.
?Then, they agree on a realistic timeline for the project.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Listen to each sentence, then record yourself saying it. Speak slowly and clearly.

Every stakeholder must approve the scope.
Cada parte interessada deve aprovar o escopo.
If you confirm today, we will proceed with the signing.
Se você confirmar hoje, nós prosseguiremos com a assinatura.
The client said the scope was too wide.
O cliente disse que o escopo era amplo demais.
We have reached two milestones this year.
Nós atingimos dois marcos este ano.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Choose the best sentence for each situation.

You make a conditional offer to a client. What is correct?
A "If you will sign, we will start."
B "If you sign this week, we will start the project."
C "If you sign this week, we start the project."
You report to your boss what the client said: "The scope is too wide." What is correct?
A "The client said the scope was too wide."
B "The client said me the scope is too wide."
C "The client told the scope is too wide."
You state a rule for the stakeholders. What is correct?
A "Each stakeholder must to approve the plan."
B "Each stakeholder must approve the plan and may request changes."
C "Each stakeholder musts approve the plan."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Record yourself answering the question. There is no right or wrong answer.

Out loud, in English, describe a real deal or project from your work. Use all five tools of the arc: say what your team has achieved (present perfect), give your opinion, state a rule with must or may, make a conditional offer with if and will, and report one thing someone said with said or told.

Survival Card -- Aula 20

1We have reached two milestones this year.Nós atingimos dois marcos este ano.
2Every stakeholder must approve the scope.Cada parte interessada deve aprovar o escopo.
3If you confirm today, we will proceed with the signing.Se você confirmar hoje, nós prosseguiremos com a assinatura.
4The client said the scope was too wide.O cliente disse que o escopo era amplo demais.
5I am accountable for this deliverable.Eu sou responsável por este entregável.
